## lattice-system

A Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization project targeting general lattice models.
This project subsumes and generalizes the earlier
[ising-model](https://github.com/phasetr/ising-model) project, progressively
covering classical spin systems, quantum spin systems, Hubbard, BCS,
CAR algebras, and eventually lattice QCD.

## Design axis: graphs, not lattices

Despite the name, the **primary combinatorial abstraction in this
library is a graph `(╬ø, E_╬ø)`** ÔÇö finite for finite-volume work and
infinite for the thermodynamic-limit / algebraic-formulation work
that is a major long-term goal ÔÇö not "a lattice". Concrete lattices
(the 1D chain, square / cubic grids, infinite chains, Ôäñ^d, ÔÇª) appear
only as specific instances such as `SimpleGraph.pathGraph`,
`SimpleGraph.cycleGraph`, products of these, or their infinite
analogues. This convention follows the standard mathematical-physics
literature on many-body systems on graphs (Lieb's theorem on
bipartite lattices, the MarshallÔÇôLiebÔÇôMattis theorem, Miyao 2021
┬º3, ÔÇª) and aligns the project with mathlib's `SimpleGraph`
foundations.

Finite-volume work uses `╬ø : Type*` together with `[Fintype ╬ø]`
when needed (e.g. for traces, partition functions, finite sums of
local terms); infinite-volume work drops the `Fintype` assumption
and uses graphs over types like `Ôäñ` or `Ôäñ^d` instead.

The bridge from a `SimpleGraph` to the pairwise coupling
`J : ╬ø ÔåÆ ╬ø ÔåÆ Ôäé` consumed by `heisenbergHamiltonian` (and similar
operators) lives in `LatticeSystem.Lattice.Graph` (`couplingOf`).
Existing chain coupling families (`openChainCoupling`,
`periodicChainCoupling`) are characterised as
`couplingOf (pathGraph _) _` and `couplingOf (cycleGraph _) _`
respectively.

## Scope

| Area | Stage | Typical references |
|---|---|---|
| Classical spin systems | Inherited from ising-model | Friedli-Velenik, Glimm-Jaffe |
| Quantum spin systems | Current focus | Tasaki, Nielsen-Chuang (cross-check) |
| Hubbard / BCS | Medium term | Tasaki 1998, Bru-Pedra |
| CAR-algebraic formulation | Medium-long term | Araki-Moriya, Bru-Pedra |
| Thermodynamic limit (infinite graphs) | Long term, **major project goal** | Simon, Friedli-Velenik, Bratteli-Robinson |
| Lattice QCD | Longest term | Aarts, Davies |

## Refactoring conventions and review criteria

A **single-source-of-truth document** for refactoring conventions
applied as the review checklist on every pull request:
[Refactoring conventions and review criteria](refactoring-conventions.html).
Topics: test methods (decide / bridge / small-exhaustive / shim /
`#guard_msgs`), module-split criteria, generic / dedup conventions,
deprecation window policy, naming / docstring rules, linter
exceptions, public-doc synchronisation.

The companion page [Deprecation window](deprecations.html) tracks
currently-deprecated public declarations, the `since` date,
recommended replacement, and earliest-removal window.

The canonical proof witnesses are `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` (PR #3891, toy coupling) and `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_bipartiteCompletePositive` (PR #3893, general bipartite `J` positive on the complete bipartite graph). These declarations are the structurally repaired replacements for the older `h_intermediate` capstones removed in PR #3917. #3850 block-diag eigenspace `= ÔèÑ` below joint per-block min, (i.6) #3851 full eig `Ôëñ 2` at min(per-block mins) given per-block `Ôëñ 1` at min, (i.7) #3854 bare `─ñ'` specialisation, (i.8) #3855 hermitianMinEigenvalue` hypothesis explicit). **Target balanced-sector PF discharge** (Issue #3739): off-diagonal anisotropic/Heisenberg agreement, real sector matrices, the Marshall-dressed shifted anisotropic sector matrix, structural irreducibility, and CollatzÔÇôWielandt PF/minimum identification now provide the balanced-sector `finrank Ôäé Ôëñ 1` input at the spin-1/2 target point and in the general spin-S target sector; the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit `h_balanced_sector_pf` callback. **Strict-gap, SU(2)-uniqueness, and MLM endpoint wrappers** now replace the explicit balanced/full equality input by the standard strict gap over all non-balanced non-empty magnetization sectors, derive that strict gap from SU(2)-endpoint global uniqueness, and then construct the SU(2) uniqueness input from the general Theorem 2.3 MLM/Casimir/PF endpoint. **D-boundary extension**: the spin-1/2 parity-block route has a dedicated `D ÔëÑ 0` version because single-ion `┬▒2` moves are impossible for `Fin (1+1)`. The general spin-S route now also has a bond-only parity reachability replacement for the single-ion branch, giving general `D ÔëÑ 0` parity-block irreducibility and the MLM/Casimir target wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general`. **╬╗=1 boundary**: at spin `1/2`, `(S^3)^2 = (1/4)I`, so `singleIonAnisotropyS D 1` is a scalar shift and `anisotropicHeisenbergS J 1 D 1` reduces to the Heisenberg Hamiltonian plus `D|╬ø|/4`; the SU(2) Theorem 2.3/MLM uniqueness transfers to target uniqueness and zero magnetization at `╬╗ = 1`. For general spin-S, the SU(2)-point wrappers cover `╬╗ = 1`, `D = 0`, and for `2 Ôëñ N` the ion-only parity reachability route gives the `╬╗ = 1`, `D > 0` endpoint wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general`. **Case (ii) bridges**: the case-(ii) path-region lemmas show the deformation path stays in `╬╗ ÔëÑ 1`, `D Ôëñ 0`; the first case-(ii) target wrappers reuse the balanced-sector PF bridge while leaving balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2` explicit; the strict-gap wrappers replace the direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input by the strict sector gap; the no-full-finrank wrappers then use sector projection and Hermitian below-min exclusion to remove the full `finrank <= 2` input once strict gap is supplied; the crossing-callback bridge compresses the remaining strict-gap derivation to a single target crossing contradiction callback; the path-callback bridge reduces that target callback to a contradiction for crossings along the case-(ii) path; the crossing-set bridge reduces the path callback to contradiction for non-empty `perMCrossingSet M Ôê® Icc 0 1`; the first-crossing bridge reduces crossing-set non-emptiness to contradiction at `sInf (perMCrossingSet M Ôê® Icc 0 1)`; the argmin-first-crossing bridge reduces the target contradiction to the sector whose first crossing minimises those `sInf` values; the first-crossing finrank bridge proves that argmin callback from a `finrank <= 2` bound at the selected first crossing; the path-global finrank bridge moves this to every `t Ôêê Icc 0 1`; the block-path finrank bridge reduces it to full-ground-energy parity-block submatrix simplicity; and the block-PF/min bridge reduces that to pathwise bare parity-block PF simplicity plus PF/min identification; and the parity-gauge sign layer introduces the case-(ii) block gauge needed to flip exactly the ┬▒2 parity-bond and single-ion moves; and the local sign layer proves strict shifted-entry positivity for transverse, parity-bond, and single-ion elementary moves; and the block reachability, block non-negativity, step-support, raw support-classification, and boundary move-set bridges reduce parity-block irreducibility to conditional irreducibility under the bipartite support-zero assumption on `J`. | The public general spin-S `2 Ôëñ N` parameter-region wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` package the general spin-S surface. The spin-`1/2` wrappers `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` and `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` now package the exact `N = 1` surface: `-1 < ╬╗ < 1`, `D ÔëÑ 0`; `╬╗ = 1`, using the scalar-shift boundary; and strict case (ii) `1 < ╬╗`, `D Ôëñ 0`. |

(`TJStepMatrixEntry.lean`): the moved-electron kinetic summand equals `1` (`tJ_cycle_hop_kinetic_summand_eq_one`, via the forward/backward/wrap dispatch), so a hop gives `Ôƒ¿KÔƒ®.re ÔëÑ 1` adjacent-swap bridge is in place** (`TJAdjacentSwap.lean`): an `AdjacentSwapStep` (exchange the values at an adjacent pair of distinct sites, `= s Ôêÿ Equiv.swap a b`) is always a `TJStep` (`adjacentSwapStep_to_TJStep` ÔÇö a hop if one site is empty, an exchange if the two are opposite spins), so `AdjacentSwapReachable` lifts to `TJStep`-reachability (`tjReachable_of_adjacentSwapReachable`); the sector connectivity then reduces to the model-agnostic combinatorial fact that two `Fin 3`-configs with equal value-counts are adjacent-swap reachable. **The reachability basics are in place** (`TJSwapReachableBasics.lean`): `adjacentSwapReachable_swap` (the value-swapped `s Ôêÿ Equiv.swap a b` is reachable), `adjacentSwapReachable_count` (reachability preserves every value-count, so it stays in-sector), and `exists_needed_value_right_of_same_counts` (equal counts + prefix agreement Ôƒ╣ the needed value `s' p` sits right of `p` ÔÇö the bubble target). **The sector connectivity is now proven** (`TJSwapReachable.lean`): `bubble_reachable` (move a value leftward to a target by NN swaps, prefix-preserving, induction on the gap) + `reach_of_agree_aux` (selection sort: bubble the needed value into the leftmost disagreement, recurse) assemble into **`adjacentSwapReachable_of_same_counts`** ÔÇö any two `Fin 3`-configs with equal value-counts are adjacent-swap reachable, hence (via the bridge) `TJStep`-reachable within a sector. **The shifted PF matrix is set up** (`TJSectorShifted.lean`): `tJSectorShifted = c┬À1 ÔêÆ M` with (`#Ôêà+#Ôåæ+#Ôåô=N+1`) + `tJSector_same_counts` (distinct sector states share all value-counts) feed the connectivity, and `tJSectorShifted_pow_pos_of_reachable` lifts a `TJStep`-reachable chain to a Ne)` witness (the `┼£┬│=┬¢` filling with `Ôåæ`'s left of `Ôåô`'s) supplies A.18's `[Nonempty]`, and **`tJEffReMatrixOnSector_perronFrobenius`** applies Theorem A.18 to `M = tJEffReMatrixOnSector` ÔÇö `M` has a strictly positive eigenvector at its lowest eigenvalue `╬╝` (the sector ground energy), with `finrank Ôëñ 1` (non-degeneracy): the unique positive PF ground state of the spin-charge-separated sector. **The sectorÔåöfull bridge is started** (`TJExpansion.lean`): `tJExpansion v = ╬ú_s v_sÔÇó\|╬ª_sÔƒ®`, `tJExpansionCoeff`, and the left inverse (`fermionSiteSpin{Plus,Minus,Z}_commute_hubbardDoubleOccupancy`, `fermionSiteNumber_commute_hubbardDoubleOccupancy`) hence with `P╠éhc`, so `n╠é_xn╠é_y` and `┼£_x┬À┼£_y` commute with `P╠éhc` state is proven** (`TJOperatorLift.lean`, `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_tJConfigOf`: `─ñ_tJ|╬ª_sÔƒ® = ╬ú_{s'} Ôƒ¿╬ª_{s'}|─ñ_tJ|╬ª_sÔƒ® |╬ª_{s'}Ôƒ®`, reassembling its sector-matrix column ÔÇö mirrors `tJEffMatrix_offdiag_nonpos`, diagonal by Hermiticity), upgrading the real eigen-equation to the complex one; axiom-free. **The variational bound on the ground energy is proven** (`TJGroundEnergy.lean`, E2 `Ôëñ` direction): `tJHamiltonian_groundEnergyAtFilling_le_of_sectorEigen` ÔÇö a nonzero real sector eigenvector `c` of `tJEffReMatrixOnSector` at `╬╝` gives `groundEnergyAtFilling ─ñ_tJ Ne Ôëñ ╬╝`, since the lifted `tJExpansion c` is admissible (hard-core `N╠é=Ne` eigenvector at energy `╬╝`, via the reusable generic `groundEnergyAtFilling_le_of_eigenvector` in `GroundSubspaceAtFilling.lean` + the `groundEnergyAtFilling_bddBelow` brick); axiom-free. **The reverse-bound crux is proven** (`TJGroundEnergyReverse.lean`, E2 `ÔëÑ`): `tJ_spinHalf_W_eigenvector_to_sector` ÔÇö a nonzero `┼£┬│=┬¢`, `N╠é=Ne`, hard-core eigenvector of `─ñ_tJ` at real `E` yields a nonzero real eigenvector of `tJEffReMatrixOnSector` at `E` (sector-supported Ôƒ╣ sector expansion Ôƒ╣ complex sector-matrix eigenvector, real on the sector by `tJEffMatrix_sector_im_zero` Ôƒ╣ real eigenvector via `matrix_eigenvec_re/im_of_complex`); corollary `tJ_sectorMin_le_of_spinHalf_W_eigenvalue`: under PF minimality, `╬╝ Ôëñ E`; axiom-free. **The full `N╠é=Ne` filling basis is set up** (`TJFillingBasis.lean`, W-compression foundation): the all-`┼£┬│` analog of the sector basis ÔÇö `TJFillingSector N Ne = {s // #Ôåæ+#Ôåô=Ne}` indexing the hard-core fixed-filling space `W`, with `tJFillingExpansion`/`tJFillingExpansionCoeff` + left inverse, the support restriction `tJ_mulVec_apply_eq_zero_of_not_filling`, and completeness `tJ_filling_completeness` (a hard-core `N╠é=Ne` vector equals its filling expansion); the orthonormal `W`-basis for compressing `─ñ_tJ` to the filling matrix `─ñ_W`; axiom-free. **The `W`-projection + compression homomorphism are proven** (`TJFillingCompress.lean`): `tJFillingEmbedding`, `tJFillingWSubmodule` (`(N╠é=Ne)`-eigenspace Ôèô hardcore), `PreservesTJFillingW`, the projection identity `tJFillingProjection_mulVec_eq_of_mem` (`T Tß┤┤` fixes `W`, via `Matrix.mulVec_mulVec` + `tJ_filling_completeness`), the compression `tJFillingCompress A = Tß┤┤ A T`, and the **homomorphism** `tJFillingCompress_mul_of_right_preserves` (`compress(A)compress(B)=compress(AB)` when `B` preserves `W`) + `preservesTJFillingW_tJHamiltonian`; axiom-free. **The A.17 operators preserve `W`** (`TJFillingSpinCompress.lean`): `preservesTJFillingW_of_commute` (commuting with `N╠é` and `P╠éhc` Ôƒ╣ preserves `W`) + submodule closure (`_smul`/`_add`/`_sub`) give `PreservesTJFillingW` for `┼£Ôü¢┬│Ôü¥`, `┼£Ôü║`, `┼£Ôü╗`, `┼£Ôü¢┬╣Ôü¥`, `┼£Ôü¢┬▓Ôü¥` (and `preservesTJFillingW_tJHamiltonian` for `─ñ_tJ`); axiom-free. **The compressed `─ñ_W`, `┼£Ôü¢ßÁàÔü¥_W` satisfy the A.17 hypotheses** (`TJFillingCompressSpinAlgebra.lean`): via the compression homomorphism + `compress` linearity (`tJFillingCompress_smul`/`_sub`/`_isHermitian`), the `W`-compressions inherit Hermiticity, the su(2) relations (`tJFillingCompress_su2_12`/`_23`/`_31`) and `─ñ_W`-commutativity (`tJFillingCompress_tJHamiltonian_commute_one`/`_two`/`_three`) ÔÇö exactly the inputs of the matrix Theorem A.17; axiom-free. **The filling embedding is an isometry + the Rayleigh bridge** (`TJFillingRayleighBridge.lean`): `tJFillingEmbedding_conjTranspose_mul_self` (`Tß┤┤ T = 1`), `tJFillingExpansion_dotProduct_self` (`Ôƒ¿T c, T cÔƒ®=Ôƒ¿c,cÔƒ®`), and `rayleighOnVec_tJFillingCompress` (`rayleighOnVec ─ñ_tJ (tJFillingExpansion c) = rayleighOnVec ─ñ_W c`) ÔÇö connecting `groundEnergyAtFilling` to the matrix `─ñ_W`; axiom-free. **The eigenvector lift is proven** (`TJFillingEigenLift.lean`, `mulVec_tJFillingExpansion_of_compress_eigen`): an eigenvector of `compress(A)` at `E` lifts (when `A` preserves `W`) to an eigenvector of `A` at `E` on `W`, via the projection identity `T Tß┤┤ = id` on `W`; plus `tJFillingExpansion_mem_tJFillingWSubmodule`; axiom-free. Remaining for E2-ÔëÑ: apply the matrix A.17 (`exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate`) to the min eigenvector of `─ñ_W` (its hypotheses are the proven compressed su(2)/Hermitian/commute), lift it (this lemma), use odd `Ne` to force `┼£┬│=┬¢` (`TJFillingSpinZDiag.lean`: `┼£┬│` is diagonal on filling expansions, `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_tJFillingExpansion`, and the only `┼£┬│=0` filling state is `0` for odd `Ne`, `tJFillingExpansion_eq_zero_of_spinZ_mulVec_eq_zero`) Ôƒ╣ `╬╝ Ôëñ hermitianMinEigenvalue ─ñ_W`, identify `groundEnergyAtFilling = hermitianMinEigenvalue ─ñ_W` via the bridge, completing `groundEnergyAtFilling = ╬╝`. **E2 is now COMPLETE** (`TJGroundEnergyGe.lean`): `tJ_perronFrobeniusMin_le_hermitianMinEigenvalue` (the W-restricted A.17 application), `tJ_groundEnergyAtFilling_ge_of_sectorMin` (`╬╝ Ôëñ groundEnergyAtFilling`), and the capstone `tJHamiltonian_groundEnergyAtFilling_eq_perronFrobeniusMin` (`groundEnergyAtFilling = ╬╝`, with the strictly-positive PF eigenvector `v` at `╬╝`) ÔÇö resting only on Theorem A.17's documented `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` axiom. **E3a (the SU(2) upper-bound seed, `TJGroundSpinHalfFinrank.lean`) is now COMPLETE**: `tJ_groundSubmodule_spinHalf_finrank_le_one` ÔÇö `finrank Ôäé (groundSubmoduleAtFilling ─ñ_tJ Ne Ôèô (┼£┬│=┬¢)) Ôëñ 1` ÔÇö via `groundEnergyAtFilling = ╬╝`, the PF real `finrank Ôëñ 1`, the realÔåöcomplex eigenspace bridge `matrix_complex_eigenspace_finrank_le_one_of_real`, and the injective `Ôäé`-linear embedding `tJExpansionCoeffÔéù` (`╬ª Ôåª tJExpansionCoeff ╬ª`, injective by sector-support) of the block into the complex sector eigenspace at `╬╝`; supporting lemma `tJ_spinHalf_W_complexSectorEigen` (a block element's coefficient vector is a complex sector-matrix eigenvector at `╬╝`); no new axioms. **E3b PR1 (`TJSectorRaise.lean`, toward lifting the PF vector to a highest weight) is COMPLETE**: the single-site raising operator `┼£Ôü║_x = ─ëÔÇá_{xÔåæ}─ë_{xÔåô}` is **sign-free** on the sector basis ÔÇö `fermionSiteSpinPlus_mulVec_tJConfigOf_of_down` (`s x=Ôåô Ôƒ╣ ┼£Ôü║_x|╬ª_sÔƒ® = |╬ª_{s with xÔåªÔåæ}Ôƒ®` with net JordanÔÇôWigner sign `+1`, since the adjacent orbitals `(xÔåæ,xÔåô)=(2x,2x+1)` with `2x` empty collapse the two strings) + `fermionSiteSpinPlus_mulVec_tJConfigOf_of_not_down` (`s xÔëáÔåô Ôƒ╣ ┼£Ôü║_x|╬ª_sÔƒ®=0`) + the config identity `tJConfigOf_update_raise`; fully axiom-free. This Marshall sign-freeness makes the iterated `(┼£Ôü║)^m` have nonnegative config coefficients, so a strictly-positive coordinate (from `v q > 0`) gives the non-vanishing `(┼£Ôü║)^((Ne-1)/2) ╬ªÔéÇ Ôëá 0`. **E3b PR2 (`TJRaisingTower.lean`) is COMPLETE**: eigenvalue tracking along the raising tower ÔÇö `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (`┼£┬│ (┼£Ôü║)^k v = (m+k)(┼£Ôü║)^k v`, via `[┼£┬│,┼£Ôü║]=┼£Ôü║`), `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (energy `╬╝` preserved, `[─ñ_tJ,┼£Ôü║]=0`), `fermionTotalNumber_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (number `Ne` preserved, `[N╠é,┼£Ôü║]=0`); so `(┼£Ôü║)^k ╬ªÔéÇ`, when nonzero, is a fixed-`Ne`, energy-`╬╝`, `┼£┬│=┬¢+k` eigenvector; fully axiom-free. **E3b PR3 (`TJRaisingTermination.lean`) is COMPLETE**: the raising tower terminates ÔÇö `fermionTotalDownNumber_mul_fermionTotalSpinPlus` (`[N╠é_Ôåô,┼£Ôü║]=ÔêÆ┼£Ôü║`), `fermionTotalDownNumber_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (`N╠é_Ôåô (┼£Ôü║)^k v = (mÔêÆk)(┼£Ôü║)^k v`), and `spinPlusPow_succ_eq_zero_of_downNumber` (`N╠é_Ôåô v = m v Ôƒ╣ (┼£Ôü║)^(m+1) v = 0`, since the `N╠é_Ôåô`-eigenvalue `ÔêÆ1` forces `(Ôåôcount+1)┬À¤ê(w)=0` per config); fully axiom-free. So for a `┼£┬│=┬¢`, `N╠é=Ne` ground state (`N╠é_Ôåô=(NeÔêÆ1)/2`), the top `╬®=(┼£Ôü║)^((NeÔêÆ1)/2)╬ª` satisfies `┼£Ôü║╬®=0` ÔÇö a highest weight. **E3b PR4 (`TJHighestWeight.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `tJ_raised_highestWeight` combines the tower tracking + termination ÔÇö `╬ª` with `┼£┬│╬ª=┬¢╬ª`, `N╠é_Ôåô╬ª=m╬ª`, `─ñ_tJ╬ª=╬╝╬ª` Ôƒ╣ `╬®=(┼£Ôü║)^m ╬ª` satisfies `┼£Ôü║╬®=0`, `┼£┬│╬®=(m+┬¢)╬®`, `─ñ_tJ╬®=╬╝╬®` (for `m=(NeÔêÆ1)/2`, `┼£┬│╬®=(Ne/2)╬®`, the maximal-spin highest weight); fully axiom-free. **E3b PR5a (`TJExpansionSpinEigen.lean`) is COMPLETE**: the lifted ground vector's sector eigenvalues ÔÇö `tJSpinHalfFillingSector_down_count` (every sector state has `#Ôåô=(NeÔêÆ1)/2`), `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_tJExpansion` (`┼£┬│ (tJExpansion v)=┬¢(tJExpansion v)`), `fermionTotalDownNumber_mulVec_tJExpansion` (`N╠é_Ôåô (tJExpansion v)=((NeÔêÆ1)/2)(tJExpansion v)`), letting the highest-weight extraction apply to `╬ªÔéÇ=tJExpansion(ÔäéÔêÿv)` with `m=(NeÔêÆ1)/2`; fully axiom-free. **E3b PR5b-1 (`TJTotalRaiseAction.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `fermionTotalSpinPlus_mulVec_tJConfigOf` ÔÇö `┼£Ôü║_tot |╬ª_sÔƒ® = ╬ú_{x:s x=Ôåô} |╬ª_{s with xÔåªÔåæ}Ôƒ®` (the sign-free expansion, every term `+1`), the keystone for the config-nonnegativity argument toward `(┼£Ôü║)^((NeÔêÆ1)/2)╬ªÔéÇ Ôëá 0`; fully axiom-free. **E3b PR5b-2 (`TJRaiseCoeffSum.lean`) is COMPLETE**: the coefficient-sum functional `coeffSum ¤ê = ╬ú_c ¤ê_c` ÔÇö `coeffSum_basisVec` (`= 1`) and `coeffSum_fermionTotalSpinPlus_tJConfigOf` (`coeffSum (┼£Ôü║_tot|╬ª_sÔƒ®) = #Ôåô(s)`); with the uniform sector down-count, iterating gives `coeffSum ((┼£Ôü║)^((NeÔêÆ1)/2) ╬ªÔéÇ) = ((NeÔêÆ1)/2)!┬À╬úv_q > 0`, so the raised vector is nonzero; fully axiom-free. **E3b PR5b-3a (`TJFillingCoeffSum.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `coeffSum_tJFillingExpansion` (`= ╬ú_s v_s`) and `coeffSum_fermionTotalSpinPlus_tJFillingExpansion` (`= ╬ú_s v_s┬À#Ôåô(s)`) ÔÇö together the recursion `coeffSum (┼£Ôü║¤ê) = d┬ÀcoeffSum ¤ê` for a hard-core `N╠é_Ôåô`-eigenvector at `d`; fully axiom-free. Then the total-spin identification (`S_tot = Ne/2`) via the highest weight raised this way, the SU(2)-tower lower bound (paired with this `┼£┬│=┬¢` block bound for the upper bound), and the capstone replacing the axiom. The discharge lemmas add no new t-J axioms; the only inherited dependency is the A.17 sector reduction `tJHamiltonian_eigenstate_spin_zero_or_half` (which rests on Theorem A.17's documented `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` axiom), every other layer being fully axiom-free.**); **generic fixed-filling hard-core ground subspace (`GroundSubspaceAtFilling.lean`)**: `fillingHardcoreStates`/`groundEnergyAtFilling`/`groundSubmoduleAtFilling` (`H`-eigenspace at the ground energy Ôèô `N╠é=Ne` Ôèô `hubbardHardcoreSubspace`), shared by Prop 11.24 and Thm 11.26; axiom-free; **┬º11.5.3 d=1 decorated Hubbard model + Lemma 11.25 + Theorem 11.26 (AXIOMATIZED, `MetallicFerroModel.lean`, Issue #4198)**: the duplicated-internal-site lattice `╬ø=EÔê¬(I├ù{1,2})` in `Fin(3K+3)` (`decExternalSite`/`decInternalSite1/2`); localized states `decAlpha`/`decBeta1`/`decBeta2` (eqs. (11.5.7)ÔÇô(11.5.9)) + fermion ops `decA/BCreation/Annihilation` (eq. (11.5.10)); `decHopping` (`t ╬ú b╠éÔÇáb╠é ÔêÆ s ╬ú_{Ôƒ¿p,qÔƒ®ÔêêE,¤â}├óÔÇá_p├ó_q`, eq. (11.5.14)) + `decInteraction` (`U ╬ú n╠éÔåæn╠éÔåô`, eq. (11.5.13)) + `decHubbardHamiltonian`; **`axiom lemma_11_25`** (`t,UÔåæÔê×` Hubbard Ôëí `JÔåæÔê×` t-J at `¤ä=(1+4╬¢┬▓)s`, rendered as spin-structure transfer: Hubbard ground subspace is the maximal-spin `Ne+1`-multiplet **iff** the t-J one is, in the limits) + **`axiom theorem_11_26`** (d=1, `NeÔëñK+1=|E|` odd (Tasaki's `NÔëñL`) ÔçÆ `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule (3K+2) (groundSubmoduleAtFilling (decHubbardHamiltonian ÔÇª) Ne) Ne` for large `t,U` ÔÇö ground `S_tot=Ne/2`, `Ne+1`-fold; metallic when `Ne<K+1`); model axiom-free, the two limit theorems documented axioms); **generic single-particle-state operators (`SpinfulVectorOperator.lean`)**: `spinfulCreationFromVector`/`spinfulAnnihilationFromVector` (`─ê_¤â(¤å)=╬ú_x ¤å(x)─ë_{x,¤â}`), shared helper; axiom-free; **┬º11.5.4 TanakaÔÇôTasaki model + Theorem 11.27 (AXIOMATIZED, `TanakaTasakiModel.lean`, Issue #4198) ÔÇö ┬º11.5/Chapter 11 capstone**: the heavily-decorated lattice `╬ø=E├ù{1,2,3}Ôê¬I├ù{1,2}` (externals triplicated, internals duplicated), d=1 in `Fin(5K+5)` (`ttExtSite`/`ttIntSite`); special states `ttAlpha`/`ttBeta`/`ttDeltaP`/`ttDeltaI` (eqs. (11.5.19)ÔÇô(11.5.22), `├ó` with `1/ÔêÜ(3+4╬¢┬▓)`) + ops via `spinfulCreationFromVector`; `ttHopping` (`╬ú_{Ôƒ¿p,qÔƒ®}(ÔêÆs ├óÔÇá├óÔêÆt b╠éÔÇáb╠é)+uÔéü╬úb╠éÔÇáb╠é+uÔéé╬úd╠éÔÇád╠é`, eq. (11.5.24)) + `ttInteraction` + `ttHamiltonian` (finite); genuine `uÔéé,UÔåæÔê×` limit objects `ttDKernel` (`d╠é╬ª=0`, via Theorem A.12) + `ttEffectiveHamiltonian` (`uÔéé,U=Ôê×` effective hopping); **`axiom theorem_11_27`** (d=1, `uÔéü>2(|s|+2|t|)`, `K+1ÔëñNeÔëñ2(K+1)` (Tasaki `L^dÔëñNÔëñ2L^d`) ÔçÆ **in the limit** every ground state in `groundSubmoduleAtFilling (ttEffectiveHamiltonian ÔÇª) Ne Ôèô ttDKernel` has max spin `S_tot=Ne/2` ÔÇö taken as a genuine limit, not finite thresholds (Tasaki proves it only in the limit, warns finite `uÔéé,U` is not expected to work); weaker than the multiplet predicate, Tasaki claims only the spin; metallic when `1<Ne/L<2`); model axiom-free, `theorem_11_27` documented axiom ([63])) |

| Appendix A (Tasaki Mathematical Appendices) | The book-order content after Chapter 11; foundations for the deferred Chapter-11 proof discharges (frustration-free A.9/A.10, limit A.11/A.12, PerronÔÇôFrobenius A.17/A.18 ÔÇö the last largely already in `Math/PerronFrobenius*`/`CollatzWielandt*`). **Complete ÔÇö A.1ÔÇôA.28 all formalized in book order** (Issues [#4205](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4205) + [#4224](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4224), both closed; see the **status & axiomatization policy** note below the Roadmap): **Theorem A.1 (Lie product formula)** `e^{A+B} = lim_N (e^{A/N}e^{B/N})^N` (`Math/TasakiAppendixA/LieProduct.lean`, `lieProductFormula`, documented axiom ÔÇö mathlib has only the commuting case `Matrix.exp_add_of_commute`); **Lemmas A.4** (Hermitian `├é` is `ÔëÑ0` iff all eigenvalues `ÔëÑ0`), **A.5** (`├é,B╠éÔëÑ0 ÔçÆ ├é+B╠éÔëÑ0`), **A.6** (`B╠éÔÇáB╠éÔëÑ0`, and conversely every `├éÔëÑ0` is `─ê┬▓` for a *unique* PSD square root `─ê=ÔêÜ├é` via `cfc Real.sqrt`, existence+uniqueness) **proved** (axiom-free, `Math/TasakiAppendixA/PosSemidefBasics.lean`). **Theorem A.7** (Weyl monotonicity: Hermitian `├éÔëñB╠é` ÔçÆ `i`-th sorted eigenvalue `├é.eigenvaluesÔéÇ i Ôëñ B╠é.eigenvaluesÔéÇ i`; documented axiom, `Math/TasakiAppendixA/EigenvalueMonotone.lean` ÔÇö mathlib has only the extreme-eigenvalue variational principle). **Corollary A.8** (`├éÔëñB╠é ÔçÆ Tr e^├é Ôëñ Tr e^B╠é`, eq A.2.31; documented axiom, `TraceExpMonotone.lean`, resting on A.7). **Lemmas A.9/A.10** (frustration-free Hamiltonian `─ñ=╬ú─ÑÔ▒╝`, `─ÑÔ▒╝ÔëÑ╬ÁÔ▒╝`: A.9 simultaneous eigenstate ÔçÆ ground state at `╬ú╬ÁÔ▒╝`; A.10 converse) **proved** (axiom-free, `FrustrationFree.lean`). **Lemma A.11** (`├éÔëÑ0 Ôêº Ôƒ¿╬ª|├é|╬ªÔƒ®=0 ÔçÆ ├é╬ª=0`; `├é=B╠éÔÇáB╠é ÔçÆ B╠é╬ª=0`; + angular-momentum corollary `─┤┬▓╬ª=0 ÔçÆ ─┤Ôü¢ßÁàÔü¥╬ª=0`) **proved** (axiom-free, `PosSemidefKernel.lean`, over the existing `RayleighPosSemidefKernel`). **Theorem A.12** (`─ñ_v=─ñÔéÇ+vV╠é`, `V╠éÔëÑ0`: `vÔåæÔê×` finite-energy eigenstate families converging to nonzero `╬ª` satisfy the effective Schr├Âdinger eq `P╠éÔéÇ─ñÔéÇ╬ª=E╬ª` on `HÔéÇ=ker V╠é` ÔÇö documented axiom, `EffectiveLimit.lean`, weak-form + Tendsto, non-vacuous finite-energyÔçÆeffective direction). **Appendix A.2 complete.** **┬ºA.3 angular momentum**: **Lemma A.14** (su(2) ladder) ÔÇö operator identity `─┤Ôü╗─┤Ôü║=─┤┬▓ÔêÆ─┤┬│(─┤┬│+1)` (eq A.3.7) + ladder non-vanishing (`ÔêÆJÔëñM<J`, `╬ªÔëá0` ÔçÆ `─┤Ôü║╬ªÔëá0`) + raising **membership** (`─┤Ôü║╬ªÔêêH_{J,M+1}`, via `[─┤┬│,─┤Ôü║]=─┤Ôü║` + `[─┤┬▓,─┤Ôü║]=0`) + **lowering** direction (`─┤Ôü║─┤Ôü╗=─┤┬▓ÔêÆ─┤┬│(─┤┬│ÔêÆ1)` eq A.3.8, `─┤Ôü╗╬ªÔëá0` for `ÔêÆJ<MÔëñJ`, `─┤Ôü╗╬ªÔêêH_{J,MÔêÆ1}`) **proved** ÔÇö A.14 fully done (axiom-free, `AngularLadder.lean`). **Lemma A.15 (spin bound, part 1)**: norm identities `ÔÇû─┤┬▒╬ªÔÇû┬▓={J(J+1)ÔêÆM(M┬▒1)}ÔÇû╬ªÔÇû┬▓` (eq A.3.9, Hermitian ─┤^╬▒) `angRaise/angLower_normSq` + `angMom_abs_le_J` (`ÔêÆJÔëñMÔëñJ`, i.e. `JÔêÆM,J+MÔëÑ0`) **proved** (axiom-free). **Lemma A.15 (integrality)** + **Theorem A.13 (`J = n/2`)**: ladder-termination `raiseIter` (iterated `─┤Ôü║`) with `raiseIter_eigenspace`/`raiseIter_ne_zero` gives `JÔêÆM Ôêê ÔäñÔëÑ0` (`angMom_sub_mem_nat`); the gauge-reflected `(─┤┬╣,ÔêÆ─┤┬▓,ÔêÆ─┤┬│)` gives `J+M Ôêê ÔäñÔëÑ0`, so `2J=(JÔêÆM)+(J+M)ÔêêÔäñÔëÑ0`, i.e. `J=n/2` (`angMom_J_eq_half_nat`) **proved** (axiom-free, `AngularQuantization.lean`). **Theorem A.16 (SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy)**: for SU(2)-invariant `─ñ` (`[─ñ,─┤ßÁà]=0`), a joint energy eigenstate in `H_{J,M0}` yields nonzero same-energy companions in every `H_{J,JÔêÆk}` (`kÔëñ2J`) ÔÇö `ham_su2_multiplet` (energy `E` is ÔëÑ`(2J+1)`-fold degenerate), proved via raise-to-top + reflected-lowering ladder + `ham_mulVec_raiseIter` (axiom-free, `AngularMultiplet.lean`). **Theorem A.17 (spin-0/1-2 sector suffices)**: every energy eigenvalue of an SU(2)-invariant `─ñ` has an eigenstate with `─┤┬│=0` or `─┤┬│=1/2` ÔÇö `ham_eigenstate_spin_zero_or_half` (corollary of A.16+A.13; the lone simultaneous-diagonalization step `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` is a documented axiom, `AngularSpinHalfSector.lean`). **Theorem A.18 (PerronÔÇôFrobenius, real symmetric)**: a real symmetric `M` with `(c┬À1ÔêÆM)` irreducible (off-diag `Ôëñ0` + connectivity) has a nondegenerate lowest eigenvalue with a strictly positive eigenvector ÔÇö `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` (reuses the project's CollatzÔÇôWielandt PF + eigenspace simplicity; the lowest-eigenvalue identification is the variational `|w|`-argument eq A.4.1), proved (axiom-free, `PerronFrobeniusSymmetric.lean`). **Theorems A.19/A.20 (polar + SVD)**: `matrix_polar_decomposition` (`A = W C`, `W` unitary, `C` PSD) and `matrix_singular_value_decomposition` (`A = U D VÔÇá`, `U,V` unitary, `D = diagonal d` with `d ÔëÑ 0`) ÔÇö documented axioms (mathlib has singular *values* but not the constructive `Matrix`-level factorizations; statements kept in book order, `MatrixDecomposition.lean`). **Theorems A.21/A.22 (Wigner)**: `wignerAutomorphism_unitary` (linear Ôêù-automorphism of `M(H)` Ôƒ╣ `╬ô(├é)=├øÔÇá├é├ø`) + `wignerAutomorphism_antiunitary` (antilinear Ôƒ╣ `├øÔÇá├é╠ä├ø`) + `wignerProjection` (rank-1-projection map preserving `Tr[P PÔÇ▓]` Ôƒ╣ (anti)unitary `V╠é`) ÔÇö documented axioms over `Matrix D D Ôäé` (`WignerTheorem.lean`, Issue #4224). **Definition A.23 + Theorem A.24 (states / BanachÔÇôAlaoglu)**: `IsState` (weak-Ôêù-continuous `¤å : WeakDual Ôäé A` with `¤å 1 = 1`, `0 Ôëñ ¤å(aÔÇáa)`) over an abstract unital C*-algebra + `stateSpace_isCompact` (state space weak-Ôêù compact, documented axiom) ÔÇö `CStarState.lean`. **Definitions A.25/A.27 + Theorem A.26 (ground states)**: dynamics modelled by a derivation `╬┤=[─ñ,┬À]`; `IsGroundState` (`0 Ôëñ ¤ë(aÔÇá┬À╬┤a)`) + `HasNonzeroGap` (`╬│>0`, `¤ë(aÔÇá╬┤a) ÔëÑ ╬│¤ë(aÔÇáa)` on `¤ë(a)=0`) + `groundState_variational` (ground state Ôƒ║ `¤ë(─ñ_L)` least over states agreeing outside ╬ø_L, documented axiom) ÔÇö `CStarGroundState.lean`. **Theorem A.28 (GNS construction)**: `gns_construction` ÔÇö every state `¤ü` on a C*-algebra has a GNS triple `(H_¤ü, ¤Ç_¤ü, ╬®_¤ü)` (`Ôêù`-representation `¤Ç : A ÔåÆÔïåÔéÉ[Ôäé] (HÔåÆL H)` + cyclic `╬®`) with `¤ü(├é)=Ôƒ¿╬®,¤Ç(├é)╬®Ôƒ®` and `{¤Ç(├é)╬®}` dense; documented axiom (mathlib has the GNS machinery), `CStarGNS.lean`. **┬ºA.6ÔÇô┬ºA.7 (Wigner / states / ground states / GNS) complete ÔåÆ Appendix A complete.** Prove-first where mathlib supports, axiomatize-first for the heavy analytic results, strict book order (see the status & axiomatization-policy note below). |

### Appendix A: status and axiomatization policy

Tasaki's **Appendix A is fully formalized in book order (A.1ÔÇôA.28)**, and the
entire Tasaki text up to and including Chapter 11 (┬º11.5) plus this appendix is
now covered. The appendix splits into two kinds of items:

- **Proved (axiom-free)** ÔÇö the linear-algebra and angular-momentum core that
  `mathlib` supports directly: **A.4ÔÇôA.6** (positive-semidefinite basics + unique
  PSD square root), **A.9ÔÇôA.11** (frustration-free Hamiltonians, energy-form
  kernels), **A.13ÔÇôA.16** (angular-momentum quantization `J = n/2`, the ladder,
  and SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy), and **A.18** (PerronÔÇôFrobenius for a real
  symmetric matrix, via the project's CollatzÔÇôWielandt development and the
  variational `|w|`-argument).
- **Documented axioms (faithful statements, deferred proofs)** ÔÇö **A.1** (Lie
  product formula), **A.7/A.8** (Weyl eigenvalue monotonicity, trace-exp
  monotonicity), **A.12** (strong-coupling effective Hamiltonian limit), the
  simultaneous-diagonalization step of **A.17**, **A.19/A.20** (polar / singular-
  value decompositions), and the operator-algebraic **A.21ÔÇôA.28** (Wigner's
  theorem, states, BanachÔÇôAlaoglu, ground states of infinite systems, the GNS
  construction).

**Value judgment / policy.** The documented axioms above are kept as *faithful,
book-order statements* ÔÇö they record exactly what Tasaki proves ÔÇö but they are
**not active proof targets** of this project. The heavy functional-analytic and
operator-algebraic results (states on the quasi-local C\*-algebra, weak-Ôêù
compactness, ground states, GNS, Wigner) belong to a dedicated operator-algebra /
functional-analysis development; such a development may well be carried out
**separately** (for instance contributed to `mathlib`), and these axioms simply
**wait for that implementation**. Accordingly the project's policy is to
**axiomatize only the appendix results that Tasaki's formalized main development
actually uses**, to **prove** the remaining ones where `mathlib` provides the
tools, and otherwise to leave a faithful axiom in place rather than invest in a
large bespoke operator-algebra build whose natural home is elsewhere. The
`#print axioms` of every theorem in the repository makes the precise dependency
on these documented axioms auditable.

## Formalized theorems

All items below are formally proved with **zero `sorry`**. Full
mathematical statements and proof sketches are in
[`tex/proof-guide.tex`](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/blob/main/tex/proof-guide.tex).

### Single-site Pauli operators

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬º2.1 eq. (2.1.8), p. 15. Cross-checked with Nielsen-Chuang
┬º2.1.3 Figure 2.2 (pp. 65-66, definitions), Ex. 2.19 (p. 70,
Hermiticity), Ex. 2.41 (p. 78, `(¤â^╬▒)┬▓ = I` and anticommutation),
Ex. 2.40 (p. 77, commutator, whence the cyclic products).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `pauliX/Y/Z_isHermitian` | `(¤â^╬▒)ÔÇá = ¤â^╬▒` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX/Y/Z_mul_self` | `(¤â^╬▒)┬▓ = I` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_mul_pauliY` etc. | `¤â^x ¤â^y = i┬À¤â^z` (cyclic) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_anticomm_pauliY` etc. | `¤â^╬▒ ¤â^╬▓ + ¤â^╬▓ ¤â^╬▒ = 0` (╬▒ Ôëá ╬▓) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |

### ZÔéé ├ù ZÔéé representation (Tasaki ┬º2.1 eqs. (2.1.27)-(2.1.34))

The ZÔéé ├ù ZÔéé structure is proved across files:
- S = 1/2 (projective, eq. (2.1.31)): `spinHalfRot*_pi_sq = -1`, anticommutation, products.
- S = 1 (genuine, eq. (2.1.27)): `spinOnePiRot*_sq = 1`, commutation.

See `Quantum/Z2Z2.lean` for the unified documentation.

### Spin-`S` operators (general S ÔëÑ 0, parameterised by `N = 2S : Ôäò`)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body Systems*, ┬º2.1 Problem 2.1.a (p. 15) and solution S.1 (p. 493).

Generic spin-`S` operators live on `Matrix (Fin (N + 1)) (Fin (N + 1)) Ôäé`, with `N = 2S : Ôäò` (so `N = 1` Ôçö `S = 1/2`, `N = 2` Ôçö `S = 1`). Tracking issue #458 (Tasaki ┬º2.1 P1d''' general-S generalisation).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinSOp3` | `┼£^(3) := diag(N/2, N/2 - 1, ÔÇª, -N/2)` for `N : Ôäò` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus` / `spinSOpMinus` | raising/lowering operators with matrix entries `ÔêÜ(k┬À(N ÔêÆ k + 1))` (resp. `ÔêÜ((N ÔêÆ k)(k + 1))`) on the immediate sub/super-diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp1` / `spinSOp2` | `┼£^(1) := (┼£^+ + ┼£^-) / 2`, `┼£^(2) := (┼£^+ ÔêÆ ┼£^-) / (2 i)` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_apply_top` / `_bottom` | `┼£^+` annihilates `\|NÔƒ®` (highest weight); `┼£^-` annihilates `\|0Ôƒ®` (lowest weight) ÔÇö the ladder boundaries | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp{Plus,Minus}` | **Cartan relations** `[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^+] = ┼£^+` and `[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^-] = -┼£^-`: `┼£^┬▒` shift the magnetic quantum number by `┬▒1`. Proved entry-wise via `Matrix.diagonal_mul` / `mul_diagonal` (╬▓-2 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Algebra.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_conjTranspose` / `spinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | adjointness `(┼£^+)ß┤┤ = ┼£^-`, `(┼£^-)ß┤┤ = ┼£^+`, and Hermiticity of `┼£^{(╬▒)}` for `╬▒ Ôêê {1, 2, 3}` (╬▓-3 of Issue #458). The ladder adjointness follows from the matrix-entry symmetry; `┼£^{(1)}` and `┼£^{(2)}` use the `1/2` and `1/(2i)` self-conjugacy facts | `Quantum/SpinS/Hermitian.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj` / `spinSOp3_sub_smul_mul_diagProj` / `_self_mul_diagProj` | the **diagonal projector** `P_k := diag(╬┤_{i,k})` and the eigenvalue-action lemma `(┼£^{(3)} ÔêÆ ╬╗_j ÔÇó 1) ┬À P_k = (╬╗_k ÔêÆ ╬╗_j) ┬À P_k`, with the annihilation specialisation at `j = k` (╬▓-4 of Issue #458). Foundation for the Lagrange-interpolation polynomial decomposition: each `P_k` will be expressed as `ÔêÅ_{j Ôëá k} (┼£^{(3)} ÔêÆ ╬╗_j ÔÇó 1) / (╬╗_k ÔêÆ ╬╗_j)` in a follow-up PR | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProj.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mul_diagProj` / `diagProj_mul_spinSOp3` / `spinSOp3_commute_diagProj` | the **eigenvalue equation** `┼£^{(3)} ┬À P_k = ╬╗_k ┬À P_k` (where `╬╗_k = (N : Ôäé)/2 ÔêÆ k`), with the symmetric `P_k ┬À ┼£^{(3)} = ╬╗_k ┬À P_k` and the resulting commutativity. Both are diagonal-times-diagonal computations (╬▓-5 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Lagrange.lean` |
| `mul_diagProj_apply` / `diagProj_mul_apply` / `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mul_diagProj_apply` | **Column/row selection** by the diagonal projector: `(A ┬À P_k)[i, j] = A[i, k]` if `j = k` else `0` (and dually for `P_k ┬À A`). Specialised to `┼£^┬▒` produces off-diagonal matrix units (╬▓-6 of Issue #458) ÔÇö the building blocks of the polynomial decomposition theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderProj.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_mul_spinSOpMinus` | **Ladder recursion**: `┼£^+ ┬À P_{k+1} ┬À ┼£^- = (k + 1)(N ÔêÆ k) ┬À P_k`. The triple product collapses to a non-zero scalar multiple of `P_k`. Iterating from the lowest-weight projector `P_N` (itself a polynomial in `┼£^{(3)}`) yields every diagonal projector as a polynomial in `{1╠é, ┼£^{(╬▒)}}` (╬▓-7 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderRecursion.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_first` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_last` | **Ladder boundaries**: `┼£^+ ┬À P_0 = 0` (top of ladder) and `┼£^- ┬À P_N = 0` (bottom). The first/last columns of `┼£^┬▒` vanish, so multiplication by `P_{0/N}` (which selects that column) gives `0`. Terminate the recursion at the highest- and lowest-weight projectors (╬▓-8 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderBoundary.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_isHermitian` / `sum_spinSDiagProj_eq_one` | **Hermiticity** of each `P_k`, and the **resolution of the identity** `╬ú_k P_k = 1`. The latter is the cornerstone for the matrix-unit decomposition: combined with the off-diagonal matrix units (╬▓-6, ╬▓-7), gives every matrix in `M_{N+1}(Ôäé)` (╬▓-9 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjProperties.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_eq_single` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_eq_single` | **Off-diagonal matrix-unit decomposition**: `┼£^+ ┬À P_{i+1} = ÔêÜ((i+1)(NÔêÆi)) ┬À E_{i, i+1}` and `┼£^- ┬À P_i = ÔêÜ((NÔêÆi)(i+1)) ┬À E_{i+1, i}`, where `E_{a, b} := Matrix.single a b 1` is the (a, b) matrix unit (╬▓-10 of Issue #458). These are the simplest off-diagonal matrix units expressible via `┼£^┬▒` ladder action on diagonal projectors | `Quantum/SpinS/OffDiagUnit.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_sq_eq_diagonal` | `(┼£^{(3)})┬▓ = diag((N/2 ÔêÆ i)┬▓)`. Step toward the **Casimir identity** `(┼£^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(2)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(3)})┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬À 1` for general spin (╬▓-11 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Op3Square.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_spinSOpMinus_eq_diagonal` | `┼£^+ ┬À ┼£^- = diag((i + 1)(N ÔêÆ i))`. The product is diagonal because `┼£^+[i, l] ┬À ┼£^-[l, j]` is non-zero only when `l = i + 1 = j + 1`, forcing `i = j`. Combined with the analogous `┼£^- ┬À ┼£^+` and `(┼£^{(3)})┬▓`, this assembles the Casimir identity (╬▓-12 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PlusMinusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOpMinus_mul_spinSOpPlus_eq_diagonal` | `┼£^- ┬À ┼£^+ = diag(i ┬À (N ÔêÆ i + 1))`. Symmetric to ╬▓-12 with `┼£^┬▒` swapped (╬▓-13 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/MinusPlusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_sq_add_spinSOp2_sq` / `spinSOp_total_squared` | **Casimir identity** for general spin-`S`: `(┼£^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(2)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(3)})┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬À 1`, equivalent to `S(S+1) ┬À 1` for `S = N/2`. Proves the irreducible spin-`S` representation has Casimir eigenvalue `S(S+1)` (Schur's lemma). The intermediate identity `(┼£^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼£^{(2)})┬▓ = (1/2)(┼£^+ ┬À ┼£^- + ┼£^- ┬À ┼£^+)` is proved using `module` (╬▓-14 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Casimir.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mulVec_basis` | spin-`S` eigenstate equation `┼£^{(3)} ┬À \|kÔƒ® = (N/2 ÔêÆ k) ┬À \|kÔƒ®`, where `\|kÔƒ® := Pi.single k 1` is the `k`-th unit basis vector of `(Fin (N + 1) ÔåÆ Ôäé)` (╬▓-15 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Eigenstates.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mulVec_basis` / `spinSOpMinus_mulVec_basis` | **Ladder action on basis vectors**: `┼£^+ ┬À \|kÔƒ® = ÔêÜ(k(N ÔêÆ k + 1)) ┬À \|k ÔêÆ 1Ôƒ®` for `k ÔëÑ 1`, and `┼£^- ┬À \|kÔƒ® = ÔêÜ((N ÔêÆ k)(k + 1)) ┬À \|k + 1Ôƒ®` for `k Ôëñ N ÔêÆ 1`. The standard SU(2) ladder relations on the magnetic-quantum-number basis (╬▓-16 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderStates.lean` |
| `spinSOp_total_squared_mulVec_basis` | **Casimir eigenvalue on basis**: `(┼£)┬▓ ┬À \|kÔƒ® = (N(N+2)/4) ┬À \|kÔƒ®`. Direct consequence of `(┼£)┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬À 1` (╬▓-14) ÔÇö every basis vector has the universal Casimir eigenvalue, reflecting that the spin-`S` representation is a single irreducible (Schur) (╬▓-17 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirEigenvalue.lean` |
| `spinSOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}_commute_total_squared` | **Casimir invariance**: each `┼£^{(╬▒)}` and `┼£^┬▒` commutes with the Casimir `(┼£)┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬À 1`. Direct consequence of the scalar nature of the Casimir (╬▓-18 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirInvariance.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_commutator_spinSOpMinus` | **Third Cartan relation**: `[┼£^+, ┼£^-] = 2 ┬À ┼£^{(3)}`. Combines ╬▓-12 (`┼£^+ ┬À ┼£^- = diag((i + 1)(N ÔêÆ i))`) and ╬▓-13 (`┼£^- ┬À ┼£^+ = diag(i (N ÔêÆ i + 1))`); the difference `(i+1)(N-i) ÔêÆ i(N-i+1) = N ÔêÆ 2i = 2(N/2 ÔêÆ i)` matches `2 ┬À ┼£^{(3)}` (╬▓-19 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Cartan3.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_commutator_spinSOp2` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}] = i ┬À ┼£^{(3)}`. Derived from the Cartan relations by algebraic manipulation through `┼£^{(1)} = (┼£^+ + ┼£^-)/2` and `┼£^{(2)} = (┼£^+ ÔêÆ ┼£^-)/(2i)`: `(P+Q)(P-Q) ÔêÆ (P-Q)(P+Q) = -2[P, Q] = -4 ┼£^{(3)}`, then scalar simplification `-1/I = I` (╬▓-20 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator.lean` |
| `spinSOp2_commutator_spinSOp3` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}] = i ┬À ┼£^{(1)}`. Derived from Cartan ╬▓-2 (`[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^┬▒] = ┬▒┼£^┬▒`) via `┼£^{(2)} = (┼£^+ ÔêÆ ┼£^-)/(2i)`: the commutator reduces to `(1/(2i)) (-┼£^+ ÔêÆ ┼£^-) = (-1/(2i)) ┬À 2 ┼£^{(1)} = i ┬À ┼£^{(1)}` (╬▓-21 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator23.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp1` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼£^{(3)}, ┼£^{(1)}] = i ┬À ┼£^{(2)}`. Derived from Cartan ╬▓-2 via `┼£^{(1)} = (┼£^+ + ┼£^-)/2`: the commutator reduces to `(1/2) (┼£^+ ÔêÆ ┼£^-) = (1/2) ┬À (2i) ┬À ┼£^{(2)} = i ┬À ┼£^{(2)}`. Together with ╬▓-20 and ╬▓-21 this completes the standard SU(2) commutator algebra (Tasaki eq. (2.1.1)) for spin-`S` operators (╬▓-22 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator31.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mul_self` / `spinSDiagProj_mul_of_ne` | **Idempotence and orthogonality** of the diagonal projectors `P_k = |kÔƒ®Ôƒ¿k|`: `P_k ┬À P_k = P_k` and `P_i ┬À P_j = 0` for `i Ôëá j`. Combined with ╬▓-9 (`Ôêæ_k P_k = 1`) this gives the spectral decomposition of the identity for `┼£^{(3)}` (╬▓-23 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjOrtho.lean` |
| `aeval_diagonal` | **Polynomial evaluation at a diagonal matrix**: `aeval (Matrix.diagonal v) p = Matrix.diagonal (fun i => p.eval (v i))`. Foundational lemma for the Lagrange-interpolation step (╬▓-25+) ÔÇö lets us pull a polynomial in `┼£^{(3)}` (a diagonal matrix) through to its scalar action on each diagonal entry. Proof by `Polynomial.induction_on'` on monomials and addition (╬▓-24 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AevalDiagonal.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_eq_lagrange_aeval` | **Lagrange-interpolation formula for `P_k`**: `P_k = aeval (┼£^{(3)}) (Lagrange.basis Finset.univ (spinSOp3Eigen N) k)`, equivalently `P_k = ÔêÅ_{j Ôëá k} (┼£^{(3)} ÔêÆ ╬╗_j ÔÇó 1)/(╬╗_k ÔêÆ ╬╗_j)` with `╬╗_j = (N : Ôäé)/2 ÔêÆ j.val`. Combines ╬▓-24 (`aeval` of diagonal) with mathlib's `Lagrange.eval_basis_self` / `eval_basis_of_ne`. Each diagonal projector is therefore a **polynomial in `┼£^{(3)}`**, which combined with ╬▓-9 (`Ôêæ P_k = 1`) gives an explicit polynomial decomposition of `1╠é` (╬▓-25 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LagrangeFormula.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_eq_one_add_I_smul_two` / `spinSOpMinus_eq_one_sub_I_smul_two` | **Inversion of the Cartesian definition**: `┼£^+ = ┼£^{(1)} + i ┬À ┼£^{(2)}` and `┼£^- = ┼£^{(1)} ÔêÆ i ┬À ┼£^{(2)}`. The defining identities `┼£^{(1)} = (1/2)(┼£^+ + ┼£^-)`, `┼£^{(2)} = (1/(2i))(┼£^+ ÔêÆ ┼£^-)` invert to express the ladder operators as **linear combinations** of the Hermitian Cartesian spin operators (╬▓-26 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMAsOneTwo.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin_spinSOp3` / `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin` | **Diagonal projectors live in the algebra generated by the spin operators**: `P_k Ôêê Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼£^{(3)}}` (and a fortiori `P_k Ôêê Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`). Promotes the Lagrange-interpolation formula (╬▓-25) from "polynomial-evaluation" form to "subalgebra-membership" form via `Algebra.adjoin_singleton_eq_range_aeval` (╬▓-27 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/ProjMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mem_adjoin` / `spinSOpMinus_mem_adjoin` | **Ladder operators live in `Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**: `┼£^+, ┼£^- Ôêê Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`. Direct consequence of ╬▓-26 (`┼£^┬▒ = ┼£^{(1)} ┬▒ i ┬À ┼£^{(2)}`) and the fact that subalgebras are closed under `Ôäé`-linear combinations (╬▓-28 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Immediate-neighbor matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**: `E_{i, i+1}, E_{i+1, i} Ôêê Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`. Combines ╬▓-10 (`┼£^+ ┬À P_{i+1} = ÔêÜ((i+1)(N-i)) ┬À E_{i, i+1}`) with ╬▓-27 (`P_k Ôêê adjoin`) and ╬▓-28 (`┼£^┬▒ Ôêê adjoin`); the ladder coefficient is non-zero on the valid range and the subalgebra is closed under multiplication and scalar `smul`. Step toward expressing every matrix unit as a polynomial in `{┼£^{(╬▒)}}` (╬▓-29 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/NeighborUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_offset_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_offset_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Arbitrary stride-(k+1) matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**: `E_{i, i+(k+1)}, E_{i+(k+1), i} Ôêê Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}` for any `k`. Induction on `k`: base case is ╬▓-29; inductive step chains via `Matrix.single_mul_single_same` (`E_{i,j} ┬À E_{j,k} = E_{i,k}`) (╬▓-30 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/OffsetUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_single_mem_adjoin` | **Every matrix unit `E_{i,j}` lives in `Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼£^{(╬▒)}}`**. Three-case split: `i = j` reduces to ╬▓-27 via `Matrix.diagonal_single` (`E_{i,i} = P_i`); `i.val < j.val` is ╬▓-30 upper; `j.val < i.val` is ╬▓-30 lower. Last building block before the spanning theorem (╬▓-31 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllUnitsAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_mem_adjoin` / **`spinS_adjoin_eq_top`** | **­ƒÄ» Tasaki ┬º2.1 Problem 2.1.a (P1d''') general-`S`**: every operator on the `(2S+1)`-dimensional spin-`S` Hilbert space `Ôäé^{N+1}` is a polynomial in `{1╠é, ┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}}`. Equivalently, `Algebra.adjoin Ôäé {┼£^{(1)}, ┼£^{(2)}, ┼£^{(3)}} = Ôèñ`. Proof: every matrix unit `E_{i,j} Ôêê adjoin` (╬▓-31), so by the entry-wise decomposition `M = Ôêæ_{i,j} M_{i,j} ÔÇó E_{i,j}` (`Matrix.matrix_eq_sum_single`) and the linearity of the subalgebra, every matrix is in the adjoin (╬▓-32 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/SpanningTheorem.lean` |
| `ManyBodyOpS` / `onSiteS` / `spinSSiteOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}` | **Multi-site spin-`S` operator space** indexed by configurations `¤â : ╬ø ÔåÆ Fin (N + 1)`, with the site-embedded operator `onSiteS i A` acting as `A` on site `i` and as the identity elsewhere; site-specialised `┼£_i^{(╬▒)}, ┼£_i^┬▒`. Hermiticity preservation `onSiteS_isHermitian` lifts from single-site to multi-site (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Phase B-╬▓ ╬▓-3a, Issue #412) | `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSite.lean` |

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body

| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki ┬º2.3): for real coupling `J` (`conj(J(x,y)) = J(x,y)`), `╬ÿ╠é_tot ((H J) v) = (H J)(╬ÿ╠é_tot v)`. | `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_openChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_periodicChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareTorusHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_cubicLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` | concrete time-reversal invariance: the open / periodic chain, the 2D open square / torus, and the 3D cubic Heisenberg Hamiltonians all commute with `╬ÿ╠é_tot` for any real coupling `J : ÔäØ`. | `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_triplet_zero` | the triplet `m = 0` state `|ÔåæÔåôÔƒ® + |ÔåôÔåæÔƒ®` is **anti-invariant** under `╬ÿ╠é_tot`: `╬ÿ╠é_tot (|ÔåæÔåôÔƒ® + |ÔåôÔåæÔƒ®) = -(|ÔåæÔåôÔƒ® + |ÔåôÔåæÔƒ®)` (the symmetric combination picks up a minus sign from the per-basis-vector flip) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |

### Multi-body operator space (abstract lattice)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬º2.2, pp. 21-26 (tensor-product Hilbert space and site-local
operators). The lattice `╬ø` is an arbitrary finite set with decidable
equality; specializing to `╬ø = Fin N` recovers an `N`-site chain.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `ManyBodyOp ╬ø` | `Matrix (╬ø ÔåÆ Fin 2) (╬ø ÔåÆ Fin 2) Ôäé` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite i A` | site-embedded operator at `i : ╬ø` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_isHermitian` | `A.IsHermitian ÔåÆ (onSite i A).IsHermitian` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_add`, `onSite_sub`, `onSite_zero`, `onSite_smul`, `onSite_one` | linearity of the site embedding and `onSite i 1 = 1` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | distinct-site commutation (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x Ôëá y`, S = 1/2) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec` / `onSite_mulVec_basisVec` | tensor-product basis states and their action under site operators (Tasaki (2.2.1)/(2.2.4)) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_same` | same-site product `onSite i A ┬À onSite i B = onSite i (A ┬À B)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_same` | same-site commutator `[onSite i A, onSite i B] = onSite i [A, B]` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | commuting Hermitians multiply Hermitian | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |

Tasaki ┬º2.5 (2.5.4) ingredient (graph-centric form). The chain / 2D / 3D `_eq_neg_one_quarter` companions reduce to this via the `_eq_neelStateOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |

| `marshallSignOf` | generic graph-centric Marshall sign `ÔêÅ_{x Ôêê A} (-1)^(¤â x)` for any finite vertex type `V`, sublattice indicator `A : V ÔåÆ Bool`, and configuration `¤â : V ÔåÆ Fin 2`. Aligns with the project-wide graph-centric design (CLAUDE.local.md) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_const_zero` | for any sublattice indicator `A`, the all-up Marshall sign is `marshallSignOf A (const 0) = 1`. Generic counterpart of `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` etc.; those are now 1-line corollaries via the `_eq_marshallSignOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig` / `marshallSignChainConfig_neelChainConfig` | the Marshall sign `(-1)^(N_A^Ôåô)` for spin-1/2 configurations on the parity-coloured chain `Fin (2K)`, encoded as `ÔêÅ_{x even} (-1)^(¤â x)`; specialisation to the N├®el configuration gives sign `+1` (no down spins on sublattice `A`). Foundational definition for the Marshall basis change underpinning the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ┬º2.5). **Deprecated** as of 2026-04-22 in favour of the generic `marshallSignOf` (the chain / 2D / 3D Marshall sign defs are kept for backward compatibility but new code should prefer the generic form) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_eq_marshallSignOf` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the chain / 2D / 3D parity-coloured Marshall signs are precisely `marshallSignOf` instantiated at the corresponding parity colouring | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig` / `marshallSignSquareConfig_neelSquareConfig` | 2D analogue: Marshall sign `ÔêÅ_{(i,j) with i+j even} (-1)^(¤â (i,j))` on `Fin (2K) ├ù Fin (2L)`; equals `+1` on the 2D checkerboard N├®el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_neelCubicConfig` | 3D analogue: Marshall sign `ÔêÅ_{((i,j),k) with i+j+k even} (-1)^(¤â ((i,j),k))` on `(Fin (2K) ├ù Fin (2L)) ├ù Fin (2M)`; equals `+1` on the 3D cubic checkerboard N├®el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down chain configurations: `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 0) = 1` and `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 1) = (-1)^K` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 2D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down checkerboard: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `2KL` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(2KL) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 3D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down cubic: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `4KLM` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(4KLM) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig` | Marshall sign under the global spin-flip on the chain: `marshallSignChainConfig K (flipConfig ¤â) = (-1)^K ┬À marshallSignChainConfig K ¤â`. Each of the K even-indexed sites contributes `-1`. Proof: `Finset.prod_mul_distrib` + helper `prod_alternating_neg_one` collapses the contributing factor product to `(-1)^K`, with the per-site identity `(-1)^((1-s).val) = (-1)┬À(-1)^(s.val)` closed by `fin_cases` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_flipConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_flipConfig` | 2D / 3D Marshall sign invariant under the global spin-flip (the contributing factor product `(-1)^(2KL)` resp. `(-1)^(4KLM)` equals `+1` for all K, L, M) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState` / `_neelChainConfig` | Marshall-rotated chain basis state `marshallSignChainConfig K ¤â ÔÇó basisVec ¤â`; specialisation at the N├®el configuration coincides with `neelChainState K` (since the Marshall sign of the N├®el state is `+1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSquareState` / `_neelSquareConfig` | 2D Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelSquareState K L` at the N├®el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallCubicState` / `_neelCubicConfig` | 3D cubic Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelCubicState K L M` at the N├®el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig_neelChainConfig` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | Marshall sign on the *flipped* N├®el configuration: `(-1)^K` (1D), `+1` (2D), `+1` (3D) ÔÇö direct compositions of `_flipConfig` and `_neelChainConfig` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState_flipConfig_eq_timeReversalSpinHalfMulti` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the Marshall-rotated *flipped* N├®el state coincides with the time-reversed N├®el state in 1D, 2D, 3D ÔÇö both sides equal the same explicit `(-1)^K` (1D) or `+1` (2D, 3D) scaled basis vector. | `marshallDressedBasis A ¤â` | **Marshall-dressed standard basis state** `:= marshallSignOf A ¤â ÔÇó basisVec ¤â` on a generic finite vertex type `V` with sublattice indicator `A : V ÔåÆ Bool` (Tasaki ┬º2.5 eq. (2.5.8), p. 41). The dressing produces a basis in which the spin-1/2 antiferromagnetic Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a connected bipartite graph has all off-diagonal matrix elements `Ôëñ 0` (Marshall sign trick), the input to the PerronÔÇôFrobenius proof of the MLM theorem | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_self` / `_of_ne` / `_apply` | pointwise rules: `╬¿╠â^¤â ¤â = marshallSignOf A ¤â`; `╬¿╠â^¤â ¤ä = 0` for `¤ä Ôëá ¤â`; explicit `╬¿╠â^¤â ¤ä = marshallSignOf A ¤â ┬À basisVec ¤â ¤ä` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one` | each factor of `marshallSignOf` is `┬▒1`, so the sign squares to `1`: `(marshallSignOf A ¤â)┬▓ = 1` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_inner` | orthonormality of the Marshall-dressed basis under the real bilinear pairing: `╬ú_¤ä ╬¿╠â^¤â ¤ä ┬À ╬¿╠â^¤ü ¤ä = if ¤ü = ¤â then 1 else 0` (combines `basisVec_inner` with `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one`) | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_mem_magnetizationSubspace` / `_zero` | the dressed basis state lies in the same magnetisation-`M` subspace `H_M = H_{¤â╠ä/2}` as the underlying `basisVec ¤â` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.10)); the `_zero` specialisation places it in `H_0` when `╬ú_x ¤â_x = 0` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | every matrix entry of the two-site spin product `┼£_x ┬À ┼£_y` is **real**: `((spinHalfDot x y) ¤â ¤â').im = 0` for all `x, y, ¤â, ¤â'`. Case analysis on `x = y` / parallel / antiparallel via the existing `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_{parallel,antiparallel}` action lemmas. **Property (i) ingredient** for the MarshallÔÇôLiebÔÇôMattis theorem (Tasaki ┬º2.5, p. 41) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_im_eq_zero` | for **real** coupling `J : ╬ø ÔåÆ ╬ø ÔåÆ Ôäé` (`(J x y).im = 0` for all `x, y`), every matrix entry of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = ╬ú_{x,y} J(x,y) ┬À spinHalfDot x y` is real: `((heisenbergHamiltonian J) ¤â ¤â').im = 0`. ÔäØ-linearity + `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_im_eq_zero` | the Marshall sign `marshallSignOf A ¤â` is **real**: `(marshallSignOf A ¤â).im = 0`. Each factor of the product is `┬▒1 Ôêê ÔäØ` (either `1` or `(-1 : Ôäé)^(¤â x : Ôäò)` with `(¤â x : Ôäò) Ôêê {0, 1}`); products of reals are real | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_im_eq_zero` | **MLM Property (i)**: for real coupling `J`, the dressed Heisenberg bilinear pairing `╬ú_¤ä \|╬¿╠â^¤âÔƒ® ¤ä ┬À (H ┬À \|╬¿╠â^{¤â'}Ôƒ®) ¤ä` is real (Tasaki ┬º2.5, p. 41 in the proof of Theorem 2.2). | `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | for any operator `M`, the dressed bilinear pairing factorises: `╬ú_¤ä \|╬¿╠â^¤âÔƒ® ¤ä ┬À (M ┬À \|╬¿╠â^{¤â'}Ôƒ®) ¤ä = marshallSignOf A ¤â ┬À marshallSignOf A ¤â' ┬À M ¤â ¤â'`. | `marshallSignOf_mul_marshallSignOf_basisSwap_of_bipartite_antiparallel` | **Marshall sign relation**: for a bond `{x, y}` crossing the bipartition (`A x Ôëá A y`) with `¤â` antiparallel at `{x, y}` (`¤â x Ôëá ¤â y`), `marshallSignOf A ¤â * marshallSignOf A (basisSwap ¤â x y) = -1`. The combined product over `╬ø` of pairwise factors collapses: outside `{x, y}` each pairwise factor is `(┬▒1)┬▓ = 1`; at the unique site in `A Ôê® {x, y}` the pair contributes `(-1)^(¤â x + ¤â y) = -1` since `¤â x Ôëá ¤â y`; the other site of `{x, y}` lies outside `A` and contributes `1` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos` | per-bond non-positivity: for `¤â Ôëá ¤â'` and any bond `(x, y)` with real non-negative `J(x, y)` supported on bipartite bonds, the contribution `marshallSignOf A ¤â ┬À marshallSignOf A ¤â' ┬À J(x,y) ┬À (spinHalfDot x y) ¤â ¤â'` to the dressed off-diagonal element has non-positive real part. | `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_re_nonpos_of_ne` | **MLM Property (ii)** (Tasaki ┬º2.5, p. 41): for real non-negative `J` supported on bipartite bonds and `¤â Ôëá ¤â'`, the dressed off-diagonal Heisenberg pairing `╬ú_¤ä \|╬¿╠â^¤âÔƒ® ¤ä ┬À (H ┬À \|╬¿╠â^{¤â'}Ôƒ®) ¤ä` has non-positive real part. Sum bond-by-bond using `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos`. | `SwapStep`, `SwapReachable` | one-step swap relation `¤â Ôåª basisSwap ¤â x y` along a graph edge `(x, y)` with `¤â x Ôëá ¤â y`; reflexive transitive closure for multi-step reachability | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_walk_of_ne` | for any `G`-walk from `x` to `y` and `¤â x Ôëá ¤â y`, `SwapReachable G ¤â (basisSwap ¤â x y)`. Walk induction with case analysis on `¤â z` at intermediate vertex (Tasaki p. 41 "Proof of Property (iii)" Lemma) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_reachable_of_ne` / `_preconnected_` | for any `x, y` reachable in `G` (or any `x, y` if `G` preconnected) with `¤â x Ôëá ¤â y`, the swap is reachable. **MLM Property (iii) ingredient** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 p. 41) ÔÇö combined with iteration over the magnetisation-difference, gives PerronÔÇôFrobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `HÔéÇIndex ╬ø` | index type `{¤â : ╬ø ÔåÆ Fin 2 // magnetization ╬ø ¤â = 0}` for the zero-magnetisation subspace `H_0`; `Fintype` and `DecidableEq` instances | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0` | real-valued matrix on `HÔéÇIndex ╬ø` with entries `Re (marshallSignOf A ¤â ┬À marshallSignOf A ¤ä ┬À (H_J)_{¤â,¤ä})` ÔÇö the matrix to which Tasaki's PerronÔÇôFrobenius proof of MLM applies | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_isSymm` | the matrix is **symmetric** for real symmetric `J` (Hermiticity of Heisenberg + realness of entries) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_offdiag_nonpos` | **off-diagonal entries are non-positive** for real non-negative bipartite `J` and distinct `¤â Ôëá ¤ä`, packaged from PR ╬▒-3's Property (ii) via `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `magnetization_basisSwap` | `basisSwap ¤â x y` preserves total magnetisation. Proof uses the identification `basisSwap ¤â x y = ¤â Ôêÿ Equiv.swap x y` (the swap is a permutation of `╬ø`); the magnetisation `Ôêæ_z spinSign(¤â z)` is invariant under such reindexing (`Equiv.sum_comp`). | `disagreementSet` / `configDist` | the set / count of sites where `¤â` and `¤â'` disagree; `configDist_eq_zero_iff` characterises configuration equality | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization` | for `¤â Ôëá ¤â'` with equal magnetisation, there exist sites `x` (with `¤â x = 0, ¤â' x = 1`) and `y` (with `¤â y = 1, ¤â' y = 0`). Pigeonhole/cardinality argument: the `(0, 1)`-disagreement and `(1, 0)`-disagreement sets have equal cardinality from magnetisation equality, and the disagreement set is non-empty for `¤â Ôëá ¤â'` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `configDist_basisSwap_lt` | swapping at sites `x Ôêê D01`, `y Ôêê D10` strictly decreases the configuration distance to `¤â'`. The disagreement set strictly shrinks (`x` newly agrees with `¤â'` after swap) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_eq_magnetization` | **Tasaki ┬º2.5 p. 42 Proposition**: any two configurations `¤â`, `¤â'` with the same total magnetisation are connected by a chain of single-edge bond swaps, on a connected graph. Strong induction on `configDist`, reducing by `ÔëÑ 2` per step via the swap pair from `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization`. **Final ingredient** for PerronÔÇôFrobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted` | the shifted matrix `B := c┬ÀI ÔêÆ M` on `HÔéÇIndex ╬ø`. Used as input to PerronÔÇôFrobenius: `B` is symmetric, has non-negative off-diagonal (sign flip of `M`'s non-positive off-diagonal), and non-negative diagonal when `c ÔëÑ M ¤â ¤â` for all `¤â`. The maximum eigenvalue of `B` corresponds to the minimum eigenvalue of `M` (the H_0 ground state of the AFM Heisenberg) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` / `_nonneg` (`_offdiag_nonneg`, `_diag_nonneg`) | symmetry and (off-diagonal / full) non-negativity of `B` under the appropriate hypotheses on `J` and `c` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap` | the off-diagonal matrix entry `(spinHalfDot x y) ¤â (basisSwap ¤â x y) = 1/2` for `x Ôëá y` and antiparallel `¤â_x Ôëá ¤â_y`. | `basisSwap_basisSwap_ne_self_of_ne_bond` | combinatorial helper: for `x Ôëá y`, `¤â_x Ôëá ¤â_y`, and `(u, v) Ôêë {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the configuration `basisSwap (basisSwap ¤â x y) u v Ôëá ¤â`. Site analysis: `¤â` and `¤â' = basisSwap ¤â x y` differ at exactly `{x, y}`, so for the iterated swap to return to `¤â`, the swap sites `{u, v}` must coincide with `{x, y}`. | `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap_off_bond_eq_zero` | for `¤â' = basisSwap ¤â x y` (with `x Ôëá y`, `¤â_x Ôëá ¤â_y`) and any `(u, v) Ôêë {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the matrix entry `(spinHalfDot u v) ¤â ¤â' = 0`. | `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_basisSwap` | the Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related configurations: `(heisenbergHamiltonian J) ¤â (basisSwap ¤â x y) = (J x y + J y x) / 2`. | `dressed_pairing_basisSwap_eq` / `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_apply_basisSwap` | the dressed Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related H_0 configurations: complex-level value `-J(x, y)` (Marshall sign trick ├ù Heisenberg formula ├ù symmetric `J`), real-part value `-(J x y).re`. | `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pos_of_basisSwap` | strict positivity `0 < B ¤â ¤ä` on swap-related H_0 configurations with positive symmetric bipartite `J`. Single-step strict positivity for PerronÔÇôFrobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedSwap.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_path` | generic matrix-power positivity from a positive path: for non-negative matrix `B` and a path `p_0, ..., p_{k+1}` with `B(p_i, p_{i+1}) > 0` on every consecutive pair, `(B^(k+1))(p_0)(p_{k+1}) > 0`. Induction on `k` using `pow_succ` + `Finset.sum_pos'`. Used to lift single-step swap positivity (╬▒-5j) to multi-step matrix-power positivity for PF irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowPath.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_pow_pos_step` | one-step extension: if `(B^m) ¤â ¤ä > 0` and `B ¤ä ¤ü > 0` for non-negative `B`, then `(B^(m+1)) ¤â ¤ü > 0`. Inductive building block for ReflTransGen-style matrix-power lifting | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowExtend.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pow_pos_of_swapReachable` | for `¤â : HÔéÇIndex ╬ø` and any `╬¥` with `Relation.ReflTransGen (SwapStep G) ¤â.val ╬¥`, there exists `m` with `(B^m) ¤â Ôƒ¿╬¥, h_magÔƒ® > 0`. Induction on `ReflTransGen`: refl gives `m = 0`, tail extends by one swap using ╬▒-5j (single-step swap positivity) and ╬▒-5l (one-step matrix-power extension). | `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isIrreducible` | **`B = c ┬À I ÔêÆ M` is irreducible** on `H_0` for connected bipartite `G` with positive symmetric real coupling supported on `G`-edges and shift constant `c > M ¤â ¤â` (strict). | `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isHermitian` | the shifted matrix is Hermitian (= symmetric for real matrices). Wraps `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` (PR ╬▒-5d) into the IsHermitian form needed by PerronÔÇôFrobenius | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_exists_pos_eigenvec_max` / `_pos_eigenvec_unique` | **PerronÔÇôFrobenius applied to `B = c ┬À I ÔêÆ M` on `H_0`**: existence of a strictly positive eigenvector `v` for some real eigenvalue `╬╝`, and uniqueness up to positive scalar. Translating back to `M`, `v` is the eigenvector for the **minimum** eigenvalue (the H_0 ground state of the AFM Heisenberg). This is the matrix-level Tasaki (2.5.4): the H_0 ground-state expansion `╬ú_¤â c_¤â \|╬¿╠â^¤âÔƒ®` with `c_¤â = v ¤â > 0` is unique up to positive scalar | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |
| `bipartiteCoupling` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian` | the Tasaki ┬º2.5 p. 40 toy Hamiltonian setup: `bipartiteCoupling A x y := if A x Ôëá A y then 1 else 0` (the unnormalised bipartite coupling), and `heisenbergToyHamiltonian A := heisenbergHamiltonian (bipartiteCoupling A)`. Real symmetric, non-negative, supported on bipartite bonds, positive on inter-sublattice pairs. Hermitian. Used in subsequent PRs to derive `S_tot = 0` for the AFM Heisenberg ground state via inner-product comparison | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonian.lean` |
| `bipartiteGraphFromA` | the complete bipartite graph on `╬ø` from sublattice indicator `A : ╬ø ÔåÆ Bool`: vertices `x, y` are adjacent iff `A x Ôëá A y`. | `bipartiteGraphFromA_preconnected` | `bipartiteGraphFromA A` is preconnected when both sublattices are non-empty. Cases on `A x = A y` (length-2 path via opposite sublattice) vs `A x Ôëá A y` (direct edge). | `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_eq_zero_of_card_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eq_zero_of_card_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_eq_totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}_of_cardNotA_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eq_totalSpinSSquared_of_cardNotA_zero` | **sublattice Ôåö total spin identities at the saturated edge case**: when `\|┬¼A\| = 0`, the complement sublattice `┬¼A` is empty, so `┼£_┬¼A^(╬▒) = 0` and `┼£_┬¼A┬▓ = 0`, and the `A`-sublattice operators coincide with the total operators: `┼£_A^(╬▒) = ┼£_tot^(╬▒)` for `╬▒ Ôêê {1,2,3}` and `┼£_A┬▓ = ┼£_tot┬▓`. Foundation for the dim-bound on the predicted GS subspace in the saturated case: `eigenspace(┼£_tot┬▓)_╬▒ Ôèå bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` whenever `\|┬¼A\| = 0` and `╬▒ = s_A(s_A+1)` (PR #2784, file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeEqTotalOfCardZero.lean`) |

Proof: any `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `s_A(s_A+1)` automatically satisfies all three joint Casimir eigenspace conditions, since (i) `(┼£_tot)┬▓` eigenvalue matches the predicted target `(s_AÔêÆ0)(s_AÔêÆ0+1)`, (ii) `┼£_A┬▓ = ┼£_tot┬▓` (PR #2784) gives the `(┼£_A)┬▓` condition for free, (iii) `┼£_┬¼A┬▓ = 0` trivially. Combined with PR #2768's `saturatedFerromagnetJointEigenspace = span(ladderIterateUp)` and finrank `2 m_max + 1`, this yields the dimension lower bound `finrank(predicted GS) ÔëÑ 2 m_max + 1 = 2 s_A + 1` at the saturated edge case, matching the Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3 prediction `2 S_tot + 1` (PR #2785, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGSEigenspaceBridge.lean`) |

Direct corollary of PR #2785's eigenspace bridge applied to the `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenspace component of the saturated FM joint eigenspace, with the identification `m_max = s_A` (forced by `\|V\| = \|A\|`). With PR #2768 (joint = span ladder) and PR #2769 (finrank = 2 m_max + 1), this gives the dimension lower bound `finrank(predicted GS subspace) ÔëÑ 2 m_max + 1 = 2 s_A + 1` (PR #2786, file `Quantum/SpinS/SaturatedFMJointLePredictedGS.lean`) |

Building block for Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3's orientation-independent total-spin magnitude `\|\|A\| ÔêÆ \|┬¼A\|\|┬ÀS` (PR #2841, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_complement_norm_eq` | **orientation independence of the imbalance-weight norm**: `ÔÇûbipartiteImbalanceWeight (┬¼A) NÔÇû = ÔÇûbipartiteImbalanceWeight A NÔÇû`. Direct corollary of PR #2841 (`_complement_eq_neg`) and `norm_neg`. Confirms the orientation-independent total-spin magnitude `\|\|A\| ÔêÆ \|┬¼A\|\|┬ÀS` predicted by Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3 (PR #2842, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightNormComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_norm_eq_mMax_of_cardNotA_zero` / `_of_cardA_zero` | **imbalance-weight norm at saturated edges = `|╬ø|┬ÀN/2`**: at `\|┬¼A\| = 0` or `\|A\| = 0`, `ÔÇûbipartiteImbalanceWeight A NÔÇû = \|╬ø\|┬ÀN/2 = m_max`. | `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_eq_zero_iff_card_eq` | **characterisation of `bipartiteImbalanceWeight = 0`**: at `N ÔëÑ 1`, `bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N = 0 Ôåö \|A\| = \|┬¼A\|`. Strengthens PR #2773's forward direction with the converse via the real-part formula and `N/2 > 0` (PR #2854, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightEqZeroIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_norm_eq_zero_iff_card_eq` | **norm-zero characterisation**: at `N ÔëÑ 1`, `ÔÇûbipartiteImbalanceWeight A NÔÇû = 0 Ôåö \|A\| = \|┬¼A\|`. Direct from `norm_eq_zero` + PR #2854 (PR #2855, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightNormEqZero.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_re_pos_of_cardA_gt_cardNotA_of_N_pos` / `_re_neg_of_cardA_lt_cardNotA_of_N_pos` | **strict sign of the imbalance-weight real part** (at `N ÔëÑ 1`): `\|A\| > \|┬¼A\|` gives `(bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N).re > 0`; `\|A\| < \|┬¼A\|` gives `< 0`. Direct from PR #2773's bridge + PR #2826's norm formula. | `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement_norm_eq` | **complement N├®el-state magnetization norm = same predicted spin**: `ÔÇûmagEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (┬¼A) N)ÔÇû = \|\|A\| ÔêÆ \|┬¼A\|\|┬ÀN/2`. The `min` is the "wrong"-orientation eigenvalue, strictly below the physical predicted value. At saturated edges where `ÔÇûbiwÔÇû = m_max`, this equals `m_max┬À(m_maxÔêÆ1)` (matching PRs #2958/#2959 alternative-orientation values) (PR #2967, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMinCanonicalComplement.lean`) |

From the bilinear expansion (PR #1058) plus diagonal `┼£_x ┬À ┼£_x = N(N+2)/4 ┬À 1` and off-diagonal `┼£_x ┬À ┼£_y ┬À \|¤â_ÔèñÔƒ® = N┬▓/4 ┬À \|¤â_ÔèñÔƒ®` for `x Ôëá y`. | `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian eigenvalue on the all-up state**: `─ñ_toy_S ┬À \|¤â_ÔèñÔƒ® = ((\|╬ø\|┬ÀN/2)(\|╬ø\|┬ÀN/2+1) ÔêÆ (\|A\|┬ÀN/2)(\|A\|┬ÀN/2+1) ÔêÆ (\|┬¼A\|┬ÀN/2)(\|┬¼A\|┬ÀN/2+1)) ┬À \|¤â_ÔèñÔƒ®`. Direct combination of closed form (PR #1056), total Casimir on `\|¤â_ÔèñÔƒ®` (existing in `AllAlignedState.lean`), and sublattice Casimirs on `\|¤â_ÔèñÔƒ®` (PR #1059) (╬│-4 step 19) | `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` (PR #1060) |

Non-negative on bipartite lattices, strictly positive when both sublattices non-empty (╬│-4 step 20) | `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` (PR #1061) |

| `basisVec_totalSpinHalfOp3_expectation` | **Spin-`1/2` arbitrary-¤â linear `┼£_tot^(3)` expectation**: `<basisVec ¤â \| ┼£_tot^(3) \| basisVec ¤â> = magnetization(¤â)/2` for any `¤â : ╬ø ÔåÆ Fin 2`. | `basisVecS_totalSpinSOp3_variance_eq_zero` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_totalSpinHalfOp3_variance_eq_zero` | **`┼£_tot^(3)` zero variance on basis states**: `<(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓> ÔêÆ <┼£_tot^(3)>┬▓ = 0` on `basisVecS ¤â` (spin-`S`) and on `basisVec ¤â` (spin-`1/2`). Direct corollary of steps 205/206 (spin-`S`) and 204/207 (spin-`1/2`); witnesses that every basis state is a sharp `┼£_tot^(3)`-eigenstate (╬│-4 step 208) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/Hamiltonian.lean` (PR #1255) |
| `basisVecS_off_diagonal_totalSpinSOp3` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_off_diagonal_totalSpinHalfOp3` | **Off-diagonal `┼£_tot^(3)` matrix elements vanish**: `<basisVecS ¤ä \| ┼£_tot^(3) \| basisVecS ¤â> = 0` for `¤ä Ôëá ¤â` (spin-`S`), and analogue for spin-`1/2`. Witnesses that `┼£_tot^(3)` is diagonal in the computational basis (╬│-4 step 209) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/Hamiltonian.lean` (PR #1256) |
| `basisVecS_dotProduct_basisVecS_of_ne` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_dotProduct_basisVec_of_ne` | **Basis orthogonality**: `<basisVecS ¤ä \| basisVecS ¤â> = 0` for `¤ä Ôëá ¤â` (spin-`S`), and analogue for spin-`1/2`. Companion to the existing diagonal `_inner_self` (= 1) lemma; together they witness that the computational basis is orthonormal (╬│-4 step 210) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` and `Quantum/SpinDot/Hamiltonian.lean` (PR #1257) |
| `basisVecS_dotProduct_basisVecS` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_dotProduct_basisVec` | **Combined ╬┤-form**: `<basisVecS ¤ä \| basisVecS ¤â> = if ¤ä = ¤â then 1 else 0` (and spin-`1/2` analogue). | `basisVecS_dotProduct_totalSpinSOp3_basisVecS` and spin-1/2 mirror `basisVec_dotProduct_totalSpinHalfOp3_basisVec` | **Combined `┼£_tot^(3)` ╬┤-form matrix element**: `<basisVecS ¤ä \| ┼£_tot^(3) \| basisVecS ¤â> = if ¤ä = ¤â then magEigenvalueS ¤â else 0` (spin-`S`); spin-`1/2` analogue with `M(¤â)/2`. | `basisVecS_expectation_totalSpinSOp1` / `_totalSpinSOp2` | **Spin-`S` transverse `┼£_tot^(1,2)` zero expectation on every basis state**: `<basisVecS ¤â \| ┼£_tot^(1) \| basisVecS ¤â> = 0` and `<basisVecS ¤â \| ┼£_tot^(2) \| basisVecS ¤â> = 0`. | `basisVecS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_expectation_general` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(┼£_tot^(1))┬▓ + (┼£_tot^(2))┬▓` on arbitrary `basisVecS ¤â` = `\|V\|┬ÀN(N+2)/4 ÔêÆ ╬ú_x (N/2 ÔêÆ ¤âx.val)┬▓`**. | `neelConfigOfS_z_eigenvalue_sq_sum` | **N├®el per-site z-eigenvalue squared sum**: `╬ú_x (N/2 ÔêÆ (neelConfigOfS A N x).val)┬▓ = \|V\|┬À(N/2)┬▓`. Both extremes `¤âx Ôêê {0, N}` give `(N/2)┬▓`. | `allAlignedConfigS_z_eigenvalue_sq_sum` | **AllAligned per-site z-eigenvalue squared sum**: `╬ú_x (N/2 ÔêÆ (allAlignedConfigS V N c x).val)┬▓ = \|V\|┬À(N/2 ÔêÆ c.val)┬▓`. | `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS` | **N├®el `┼£_tot^(3)` eigenvalue**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N) = (\|A\| ÔêÆ \|┬¼A\|)┬ÀN/2`. | `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`S` N├®el Casimir re-derived via the general formula**: `<╬ª_N├®el \| (┼£_tot)┬▓ \| ╬ª_N├®el> = ((\|A\|ÔêÆ\|┬¼A\|)┬ÀN/2)┬▓ + \|V\|┬ÀN/2` derived from ╬│-4 step 218 + ╬│-4 step 221 + ╬│-4 step 224 + `ring`. | `magnetization_neelConfigOf_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` negation relation**: `magnetization ╬ø (neelConfigOf (┬¼A)) = ÔêÆmagnetization ╬ø (neelConfigOf A)`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 231; direct from existing `magnetization_neelConfigOf{,_complement}` + `ring` (╬│-4 step 232) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1279) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N├®el**: `<╬ª_N├®el(┬¼A)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬ª_N├®el(┬¼A)> = <╬ª_N├®el(A)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬ª_N├®el(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 227 (Casimir = `(M/2)┬▓+\|╬ø\|/2`) + ╬│-4 step 232 (`M(┬¼A) = ÔêÆM(A)`): `(M)┬▓` is sign-flip invariant (╬│-4 step 233) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1280) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N├®el**: `<╬ª_N├®el(┬¼A)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬ª_N├®el(┬¼A)> = <╬ª_N├®el(A)\|(┼£_tot)┬▓\|╬ª_N├®el(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 218 + 221 + 231 + `ring`: `magEigenvalueS┬▓` is sign-flip invariant; `╬ú_x m_x┬▓` evaluates to `\|V\|┬À(N/2)┬▓` for both A and ┬¼A. | `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` `(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├®el**: `<╬ª_N├®el(┬¼A)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ª_N├®el(┬¼A)> = <╬ª_N├®el(A)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ª_N├®el(A)>`. | `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` `(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├®el**: `<╬ª_N├®el(┬¼A)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ª_N├®el(┬¼A)> = <╬ª_N├®el(A)\|(┼£_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ª_N├®el(A)>`. | `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(┼£^1)┬▓+(┼£^2)┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├®el**: equal expectations on `╬ª_N├®el(A)` and `╬ª_N├®el(┬¼A)`. | `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse Casimir invariance on N├®el**: both expectations equal `\|╬ø\|/2`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 219 (¤â-independent constant). | `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`S` linear `┼£_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N├®el**: `<╬ª_N├®el(┬¼A)\|┼£_tot^(3)\|╬ª_N├®el(┬¼A)> = ÔêÆ<╬ª_N├®el(A)\|┼£_tot^(3)\|╬ª_N├®el(A)>`. | `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`S` N├®el transverse linear expectations vanish**: `<╬ª_N├®el\|┼£_tot^(1,2)\|╬ª_N├®el> = 0`.
