Together with ╬│-4 step 91 (`<┼£_tot^(3)> = magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS`), gives the complete per-axis profile `(0, 0, (\|A\|ÔêÆ\|┬¼A\|)┬ÀN/2)` | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1288) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`1/2` N├®el transverse linear expectations vanish**: same as ╬│-4 step 241 for spin-`1/2`, derived from ╬│-4 step 215 (╬│-4 step 242) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1289) |

The single-cluster Problem 2.5.a modules below are imported from the
build root again after the 2026-05-30 orphan-module sweep had removed
the earlier implementation.  The restored concrete tip is
`Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean`, and the
minimum-eigenvalue bridge in `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianMin.lean`
keeps the abstract Hamiltonian, energy hygiene, concrete
dimer/trimer/quartet/pentamer eigenvalue formulas, and variational
upper/lower-bound consumers live in the default Lean build
(PR #4032; PR #4033; PR #4034; PR #4035; PR #4036).

| `singleClusterHamiltonianS` | **Single-cluster (star-graph) Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a, p. 38): `H = ╬ú_{j=1}^z ┼£_0 ┬À ┼£_j` on `Fin (z + 1)` with central vertex `0` and `z` leaves. Ground-state energy `ÔêÆS(1 + zS)` (╬│-5 step 243) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1290) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` | **Hermiticity** of the single-cluster Heisenberg Hamiltonian. Sum of Hermitian `spinSDot 0 j N` over `j Ôêê univ.erase 0` (╬│-5 step 244) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1291) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_zero_z` | **Edge case `z = 0`**: `singleClusterHamiltonianS 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = Ôêà` in `Fin 1`. Tasaki's formula `ÔêÆS(1 + zS)` is intended for `z ÔëÑ 1` (╬│-5 step 245) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1292) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<╬ª_Ôèñ\|H\|╬ª_Ôèñ> = z┬À(N/2)┬▓` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian. Far above Tasaki's GS energy `ÔêÆS(1+zS)` since all-up is in the maximum-`s_tot` Casimir sector, not the minimum (╬│-5 step 246) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1293) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allAligned_expectation` | **AllAligned-c expectation**: `<╬ª_aligned(c)\|H\|╬ª_aligned(c)> = z┬À(N/2 ÔêÆ c.val)┬▓`. Generalises ╬│-5 step 246 (the `c = 0` case). For `c = Fin.last N` (all-down) gives same `z┬À(N/2)┬▓` (╬│-5 step 247) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1294) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allDown_expectation` | **All-down expectation**: `<╬ª_ÔèÑ\|H\|╬ª_ÔèÑ> = z┬À(N/2)┬▓`. Direct specialisation of ╬│-5 step 247 at `c = Fin.last N` (╬│-5 step 248) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1295) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Leaf-spin operators on `Fin (z+1)`**: `┼£_R^(╬▒) = ╬ú_{j Ôëá 0} onSiteS j ┼£^(╬▒)` for axes `╬▒ = 1, 2, 3`. Sum over leaves of single-site `┼£^(╬▒)` (excludes central vertex `0`). Building blocks for the upcoming Casimir decomposition `H = (┼£_tot┬▓ ÔêÆ ┼£_0┬▓ ÔêÆ ┼£_R┬▓)/2` (╬│-5 step 249) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1296) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eq_dot_leaves` | **`┼£_0 ┬À ┼£_R` decomposition**: `H = ╬ú_╬▒ onSiteS 0 (┼£^(╬▒)) ┬À leafSpinSOp_╬▒`. Bilinear expansion `sublatticeSpinDot_eq_sum_sum`: `┼£_A ┬À ┼£_B = ╬ú_{x : A x} ╬ú_{y : B y} ┼£_x ┬À ┼£_y` connects the operator-level Casimir form to the bond-level Heisenberg expression in the toy Hamiltonian (Tasaki ┬º2.5 (2.5.10)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_eq_sum_dot` / `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_all_up` / `_all_down` / `_of_const_on` | `(┼£_A)┬▓ = ╬ú_{x Ôêê A} ╬ú_{y Ôêê A} ┼£_x ┬À ┼£_y` (specialisation `B = A` of the bilinear expansion), and the **maximum-spin Casimir eigenvalue on the all-aligned state**: `(┼£_A)┬▓ ┬À \|s s ÔÇª sÔƒ® = (\|A\|┬À(\|A\|+2)/4) ┬À \|s s ÔÇª sÔƒ®` for any `s : Fin 2`. Generalised form `_of_const_on`: any `\|¤âÔƒ®` with `¤â` **constant on `A`** is an eigenvector with eigenvalue `\|A\|┬À(\|A\|+2)/4` (relevant for N├®el-style states which are constant on each sublattice but not globally) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_sublatticeSpinDot_sum` | the MLM toy Hamiltonian decomposes as an oriented cross-sublattice spin dot product: `─ñ_toy = ┼£_A ┬À ┼£_┬¼A + ┼£_┬¼A ┬À ┼£_A`. Bridges the bipartite-bond sum (Tasaki ┬º2.5 (2.5.10)) to the operator-level Casimir form (Tasaki ┬º2.5 (2.5.11)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinDot_complement_comm` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_two_sublatticeSpinDot` | cross-sublattice symmetry: `┼£_A ┬À ┼£_┬¼A = ┼£_┬¼A ┬À ┼£_A` (each axis pair commutes by `sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute`), giving the closed form `─ñ_toy = 2 ÔÇó ┼£_A ┬À ┼£_┬¼A` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_eq_sublattice_casimir` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_casimir_diff` | **Casimir identity** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 (2.5.11)): `(┼£_tot)┬▓ = (┼£_A)┬▓ + 2 ÔÇó (┼£_A ┬À ┼£_┬¼A) + (┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` (per-axis `(a + b)┬▓ = a┬▓ + 2ab + b┬▓` via cross-commute), and the closed form (without `1/|╬ø|`) `─ñ_toy = (┼£_tot)┬▓ ÔêÆ (┼£_A)┬▓ ÔêÆ (┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | the toy Hamiltonian commutes with `(┼£_tot)┬▓` (specialisation of `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared`). The standard fact used to project the toy ground state onto a fixed `(┼£_tot)┬▓` eigenspace, underpinning the `S_tot = 0` selection of the toy ground state | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_commute_sublatticeSpinHalfSquared` / `_complement` | the toy Hamiltonian commutes with `(┼£_A)┬▓` and with `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓`. Direct consequences of the closed form `─ñ_toy = (┼£_tot)┬▓ ÔêÆ (┼£_A)┬▓ ÔêÆ (┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` and the three pairwise Casimir commutativities (PRs ╬▒-6o, ╬▒-6s, self-commute trivially). Together with ╬▒-6p, gives all four Casimir-style commutators of `─ñ_toy`, the prerequisite for the joint eigenbasis analysis on which the `S_tot = 0` selection rests | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_simplified` | explicit eigenvalue of `─ñ_toy` on the all-aligned state: the Casimir-difference form `\|╬ø\|┬À(\|╬ø\|+2)/4 ÔêÆ \|A\|┬À(\|A\|+2)/4 ÔêÆ \|┬¼A\|┬À(\|┬¼A\|+2)/4` algebraically simplifies via `\|╬ø\| = \|A\| + \|┬¼A\|` to the **product form** `\|A\|┬À\|┬¼A\|/2`. The eigenvalue is non-negative for any bipartite lattice and strictly positive when both sublattices are non-empty | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_neelStateOf` / `_complement_mulVec_neelStateOf` | sublattice Casimir eigenvalues on the **N├®el state** `╬ª_N├®el(A) := basisVec (neelConfigOf A)`: `(┼£_A)┬▓ ┬À \|╬ª_N├®el(A)Ôƒ® = (\|A\|┬À(\|A\|+2)/4) ┬À \|╬ª_N├®el(A)Ôƒ®` and `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓ ┬À \|╬ª_N├®el(A)Ôƒ® = (\|┬¼A\|┬À(\|┬¼A\|+2)/4) ┬À \|╬ª_N├®el(A)Ôƒ®`. Direct corollaries of `_of_const_on` since the N├®el configuration is constant on each sublattice (`¤â x = 0` on `A`, `¤â x = 1` on `┬¼A`); the N├®el state is simultaneously a `(┼£_A)┬▓` and `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` eigenvector at maximum-spin eigenvalues | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` |
| `mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_commute_of_mem` | generic preservation: any operator `A` with `Commute A (┼£tot^(3))` maps each magnetisation sector `H_M` to itself ÔÇö operator-level form of Tasaki ┬º2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 block-diagonalisation | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | Casimir specialisation: `┼£tot┬▓` preserves every `H_M` (since `[┼£tot┬▓, ┼£tot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `J : ╬ø ÔåÆ ╬ø ÔåÆ Ôäé` and `M : Ôäé`, `v Ôêê H_M` implies `H ┬À v Ôêê H_M` ÔÇö the operator-level statement that any Heisenberg Hamiltonian block-diagonalises against Tasaki ┬º2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 magnetisation-sector decomposition (consequence of SU(2) invariance `[H, ┼£tot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpMinus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : Ôäé`, `v Ôêê H_M` implies `┼£tot^- ┬À v Ôêê H_{M-1}` ÔÇö the standard SU(2) lowering ladder action via the Cartan relation `[┼£tot^(3), ┼£tot^-] = -┼£tot^-` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : Ôäé`, `v Ôêê H_M` implies `┼£tot^+ ┬À v Ôêê H_{M+1}` ÔÇö the SU(2) raising ladder action via `[┼£tot^(3), ┼£tot^+] = +┼£tot^+` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_two_site` | for `╬ø = Fin 2` and any `╬©`, the global rotation factors as `onSite 0 (├ø^(╬▒)_╬©) * onSite 1 (├ø^(╬▒)_╬©)` (general-╬© extension of Problem 2.2.b) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `onSite_exp_eq_exp_onSite` | `onSite x (exp A) = exp (onSite x A)` ÔÇö bridge between single-site and many-body matrix exponential. Local Frobenius normed structure + `LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional` + `NormedSpace.map_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_eq_exp` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.11): `├ø^(╬▒)_╬©_tot = exp(-i╬© ┼£_tot^(╬▒))`. Composes `spinHalfRot{╬▒}_eq_exp` (single site), `onSite_exp_eq_exp_onSite` (per-site bridge), and `Matrix.exp_sum_of_commute` (commuting-summand exp = noncommProd of exps) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_commute_of_commute` | Tasaki ┬º2.2 (2.2.12) ÔåÆ (2.2.13): `Commute A (┼£_tot^(╬▒)) ÔåÆ Commute A (├ø^(╬▒)_╬©_tot)`. Generic operator version, follows from `Commute.exp_right` after rewriting `├ø` via `_eq_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}_exp_commute_of_commute` | ladder version: `Commute A (┼£^┬▒_tot) ÔåÆ Commute A (exp(c ÔÇó ┼£^┬▒_tot))` for any `c : Ôäé` (useful for U(1) symmetry) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_eq_self_of_commute` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.13) finite form: `Commute A (┼£_tot^(╬▒)) ÔåÆ (├ø^(╬▒)_╬©_tot)ß┤┤ * A * ├ø^(╬▒)_╬©_tot = A`. Combines `_commute_of_commute` with unitarity | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_triplet_zero` | `┼£_tot┬▓ (|ÔåæÔåôÔƒ® + |ÔåôÔåæÔƒ®) = 2(|ÔåæÔåôÔƒ® + |ÔåôÔåæÔƒ®)` for `╬ø = Fin 2` (triplet `m = 0`, `S = 1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | the two-site singlet has zero `┼£_tot^(3)` magnetization | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_totalOnSite` | `[onSite x S╬▒, ╬ú_z onSite z S╬▓] = onSite x [S╬▒, S╬▓]` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

### One-dimensional open-chain quantum Ising

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬º3.3 eq. (3.3.1), p. 55 (transverse-field Ising on an open
chain). Our formalization uses the Pauli convention `¤â = 2┬ÀS` and an
explicit bond coupling `J`.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h` | `H = -J ╬ú ¤â^z_i ¤â^z_{i+1} - h ╬ú ¤â^x_i` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_isHermitian` | `H` is Hermitian for real `J`, `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot x y` | the Ising bond operator `¤â^z_x ┬À ¤â^z_y` (generic in `╬ø`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot_isHermitian` | each bond operator is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric (J : ╬ø ÔåÆ ╬ø ÔåÆ Ôäé) (h : Ôäé)` | **graph-friendly** Ising Hamiltonian `╬ú J(x,y) ¤â^z_x ¤â^z_y ÔêÆ h ╬ú ¤â^x_x` for any finite `╬ø` and pairwise coupling `J`; specialises to chain / graph / lattice via the choice of `J` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric_isHermitian` | Hermitian for entry-wise real `J` and real `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph G J h` | graph wrapper: `isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf G J) h`; double-sum convention matches Heisenberg-on-graph | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` | Hermitian for real `J, h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph G ╬▓ J h` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity (real `J, h`) and commute with the Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.sum_pathGraph_forward` / `sum_pathGraph_backward` / `sum_pathGraph_adj` | sum-decomposition helpers for `pathGraph (N+1)` adjacency: `╬ú_{x,y}` over ordered adjacent pairs = `╬ú_{i:Fin N} (f i.cs i.s + f i.s i.cs)` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `pathGraphParityColoring` / `pathGraph_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of `pathGraph (N + 1)` (`i Ôåª i.val % 2`) and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Underpins the N├®el state (`Quantum/NeelState.lean`) and the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ┬º2.5) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `cycleGraphEvenParityColoring` / `cycleGraph_even_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of the even cycle `cycleGraph (2 * K + 2)` and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Wrap-around case `(2K+1) + 1 Ôëí 0` still flips parity because the cycle length is even (odd cycles are not bipartite) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_eq_isingHamiltonianGeneric` | **generic-N bridge**: `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h = isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J/2)) h`. The proof itself is the robust regression test | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

| Lean name / location | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem/Tests/Sanity.lean` | sanity-check `example` blocks for concrete small-N instances (Pauli arithmetic, spin-1/2 ladder actions, Heisenberg / Ising Hermiticity on small chains, graph-centric bridge identities) |
| decide-based property tests (in `Tests/Sanity.lean`) | universally-quantified properties verified by `decide` on small finite types (graph adjacency symmetry / irreflexivity / connectivity on `pathGraph n` and `cycleGraph n` for small `n`); real proofs, no `sorry` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState ╬▓ J h N` | `gibbsState ╬▓ (quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h)` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_isHermitian` | the Ising-chain Gibbs state `¤ü_╬▓` is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[¤ü_╬▓, H_Ising] = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_zero` | infinite-temperature closed form `Ôƒ¿AÔƒ®_0 = (1/dim) ┬À Tr A` (independent of `J, h`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `O`, `(Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | conservation `Ôƒ¿[H_Ising, A]Ôƒ®_╬▓ = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(Ôƒ¿H_IsingÔƒ®_╬▓).im = 0` (energy expectation is real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | for Hermitian `O`, `(Ôƒ¿H_Ising ┬À OÔƒ®_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_sq_im` | `(Ôƒ¿H_Ising^2Ôƒ®_╬▓).im = 0` (energy-squared expectation real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_pow_im` | `(Ôƒ¿H_Ising^nÔƒ®_╬▓).im = 0` for any `n : Ôäò` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(Ôƒ¿A┬ÀB + B┬ÀAÔƒ®_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(Ôƒ¿A┬ÀB ÔêÆ B┬ÀAÔƒ®_╬▓).re = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsHamiltonianVariance_im` | `(Var_╬▓(H_Ising)).im = 0` (energy variance real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsing_partitionFn_im` | `(partitionFn ╬▓ H_Ising).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq` | for Hermitian `O`, `((Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®_╬▓).re : Ôäé) = Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®_╬▓` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(¤ü_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` for the Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_self_eq` | `Ôƒ¿H_IsingÔƒ®_╬▓ = -J ┬À Ôêæ Ôƒ¿¤â^z ¤â^zÔƒ®_╬▓ + (-h) ┬À Ôêæ Ôƒ¿¤â^xÔƒ®_╬▓` (energy as bond + transverse-field decomposition) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Gibbs state (Tasaki ┬º3.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬º3.3.

All theorems in this module are fully proved with **zero `sorry`**.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `gibbsExp ╬▓ H` | `exp(-╬▓H) := Matrix.exp (-╬▓ ÔÇó H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isHermitian` | `exp(-╬▓H)` is Hermitian (when `H` is Hermitian) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_zero` | `exp(-0┬ÀH) = 1` (Tasaki ┬º3.3, pp. 75ÔÇô78) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add` | `exp(-(╬▓Ôéü+╬▓Ôéé)H) = exp(-╬▓ÔéüH) ┬À exp(-╬▓ÔééH)` (one-parameter group) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add_of_commute_hamiltonians` | `exp(-╬▓(HÔéü+HÔéé)) = exp(-╬▓HÔéü) ┬À exp(-╬▓HÔéé)` for commuting `HÔéü, HÔéé` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_neg_mul_self` | `exp(╬▓H) ┬À exp(-╬▓H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_self_mul_neg` | `exp(-╬▓H) ┬À exp(╬▓H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isUnit` | `exp(-╬▓H)` is invertible | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_ne_zero` | `exp(-╬▓H) Ôëá 0` (corollary of `gibbsExp_isUnit`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_ne_zero` | `¤ü_╬▓ Ôëá 0` when `Z(╬▓) Ôëá 0` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_inv` | `(¤ü_╬▓)Ôü╗┬╣ = Z(╬▓) ┬À e^{╬▓H}` when `Z(╬▓) Ôëá 0` (general matrix inverse, generalises `gibbsState_zero_inv`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_smul_gibbsState_eq_gibbsExp` | `Z(╬▓) ┬À ¤ü_╬▓ = e^{-╬▓H}` when `Z(╬▓) Ôëá 0` (canonical rescaled identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_mul_gibbsExpectation_eq` | `Z(╬▓) ┬À Ôƒ¿AÔƒ®_╬▓ = Tr(e^{-╬▓H} ┬À A)` when `Z(╬▓) Ôëá 0` (canonical unnormalised expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_natCast_mul` | `exp(-(n┬À╬▓)H) = (exp(-╬▓H))^n` for `n : Ôäò` (exact discrete semigroup identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_two_mul` | `exp(-(2╬▓)H) = exp(-╬▓H) ┬À exp(-╬▓H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_inv` | `(exp(-╬▓H))Ôü╗┬╣ = exp(╬▓H)` (matrix inverse made explicit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_intCast_mul` | `exp(-(n┬À╬▓)H) = (exp(-╬▓H))^n` for `n : Ôäñ` (integer-power extension) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn ╬▓ H` | `Z := Matrix.trace (exp(-╬▓H))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero` | `Z(0) = Fintype.card (╬ø ÔåÆ Fin 2)` (dimension of the Hilbert space) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero_ne_zero` | `Z(0) Ôëá 0` (concrete sorry-free proof that the partition function is nonzero at ╬▓ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | for any Hermitian `A : Matrix n n Ôäé`, `A.trace.im = 0` (generic helper) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `H`, `(partitionFn ╬▓ H).im = 0` (Z is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_mul_self_trace` | `Tr(¤ü_╬▓┬▓) = Z(2╬▓) / Z(╬▓)┬▓` (purity / R├®nyi-2 entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(¤ü_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` for any `n : Ôäò` (R├®nyi-n entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero` | `¤ü_0 = (1/dim) ┬À I` (maximally mixed state at infinite temperature) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero_inv` | `¤ü_0Ôü╗┬╣ = dim ┬À I` (matrix inverse at ╬▓ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_zero` | `Ôƒ¿AÔƒ®_0 = (1/dim) ┬À Tr A` (high-temperature limit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState ╬▓ H` | `¤ü := (1/Z) ÔÇó exp(-╬▓H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_trace` | `Tr(¤ü) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_isHermitian` | `¤ü` is Hermitian | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation ╬▓ H O` | `Ôƒ¿OÔƒ® := Matrix.trace (¤ü * O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_one` | `Ôƒ¿1Ôƒ® = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_add` | `Ôƒ¿OÔéü + OÔééÔƒ® = Ôƒ¿OÔéüÔƒ® + Ôƒ¿OÔééÔƒ®` (linearity in observable) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_smul` | `Ôƒ¿c ┬À OÔƒ® = c ┬À Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®` (scalar linearity, `c : Ôäé`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_neg` | `Ôƒ¿-OÔƒ® = -Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sub` | `Ôƒ¿A - BÔƒ® = Ôƒ¿AÔƒ® - Ôƒ¿BÔƒ®` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sum` | `Ôƒ¿Ôêæ i Ôêê s, f iÔƒ® = Ôêæ i Ôêê s, Ôƒ¿f iÔƒ®` (finite-sum linearity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_commute_hamiltonian` | `[exp(-╬▓H), H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬º3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[¤ü_╬▓, H] = 0`, i.e. `¤ü_╬▓` is stationary under the dynamics generated by `H` (Tasaki ┬º3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `star (Tr(A ┬À B)) = Tr(A ┬À B)` for Hermitian `A, B : Matrix n n Ôäé` (algebraic core, Gibbs-independent) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_star_of_isHermitian` | `star Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®_╬▓ = Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®_╬▓` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq_of_isHermitian` | `((Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®_╬▓).re : Ôäé) = Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®_╬▓` for Hermitian `H`, `O` (real-cast equality) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_comm` | `Ôƒ¿H ┬À AÔƒ®_╬▓ = Ôƒ¿A ┬À HÔƒ®_╬▓` for any `A` (Tasaki ┬º3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_comm_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A` (`[A, H] = 0`), `Ôƒ¿A ┬À OÔƒ®_╬▓ = Ôƒ¿O ┬À AÔƒ®_╬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_eq_zero_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A`, `Ôƒ¿A ┬À O ÔêÆ O ┬À AÔƒ®_╬▓ = 0` (selection rule) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | `Ôƒ¿[H, A]Ôƒ®_╬▓ = 0` (conservation law) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(Ôƒ¿HÔƒ®_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H` (real energy expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sq_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Ôƒ¿O ┬À OÔƒ®_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (second-moment realness, variance precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_pow_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Ôƒ¿O^nÔƒ®_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O`, any `n : Ôäò` (all natural-power moments real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance ╬▓ H O` | `Var_╬▓(O) := Ôƒ¿O ┬À OÔƒ®_╬▓ ÔêÆ Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®_╬▓┬▓` (canonical-ensemble variance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsVariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Var_╬▓(O)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (variance is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_zero` | at ╬▓ = 0, `Var_0(O) = (1/dim) ┬À Tr(O┬▓) ÔêÆ ((1/dim) ┬À Tr O)┬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq_centered_sq` | `Var_╬▓(O) = Ôƒ¿(O ÔêÆ Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®_╬▓ ┬À 1) ┬À (O ÔêÆ Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®_╬▓ ┬À 1)Ôƒ®_╬▓` (centered-square form, `Z Ôëá 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance ╬▓ H A B` | `Cov_╬▓(A, B) := Ôƒ¿A ┬À BÔƒ®_╬▓ ÔêÆ Ôƒ¿AÔƒ®_╬▓ ┬À Ôƒ¿BÔƒ®_╬▓` (canonical-ensemble complex covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsCovariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_self_eq_variance` | `Cov_╬▓(O, O) = Var_╬▓(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_sub_swap_eq_commutator` | `Cov_╬▓(A, B) ÔêÆ Cov_╬▓(B, A) = Ôƒ¿A ┬À B ÔêÆ B ┬À AÔƒ®_╬▓` (antisymmetric part = commutator expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_left` | `Cov_╬▓(AÔéü + AÔéé, B) = Cov_╬▓(AÔéü, B) + Cov_╬▓(AÔéé, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_right` | `Cov_╬▓(A, BÔéü + BÔéé) = Cov_╬▓(A, BÔéü) + Cov_╬▓(A, BÔéé)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_left` | `Cov_╬▓(c ÔÇó A, B) = c ┬À Cov_╬▓(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_right` | `Cov_╬▓(A, c ÔÇó B) = c ┬À Cov_╬▓(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov_╬▓(c ÔÇó 1, B) = 0` and `Cov_╬▓(A, c ÔÇó 1) = 0` (when `Z Ôëá 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov^s_╬▓(c ÔÇó 1, B) = 0` and `Cov^s_╬▓(A, c ÔÇó 1) = 0` (when `Z Ôëá 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm ╬▓ H A B` | `Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) := (1/2) ┬À Ôƒ¿A ┬À B + B ┬À AÔƒ®_╬▓ ÔêÆ Ôƒ¿AÔƒ®_╬▓ ┬À Ôƒ¿BÔƒ®_╬▓` (symmetric / real-valued covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_self_eq_variance` | `Cov^s_╬▓(O, O) = Var_╬▓(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Cov^s_╬▓(A, B)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_comm` | `Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) = Cov^s_╬▓(B, A)` (symmetric in observables) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_add_left/right` | additivity of `Cov^s_╬▓` in each argument | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_smul_left/right` | scalar pull-out from each argument of `Cov^s_╬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add` | `Var_╬▓(A + B) = Var_╬▓(A) + Var_╬▓(B) + 2 ┬À Cov^s_╬▓(A, B)` (sum-of-observables variance identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_one` | `Var_╬▓(1) = 0` (when `Z Ôëá 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul` | `Var_╬▓(c ÔÇó A) = c┬▓ ┬À Var_╬▓(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul_one` | `Var_╬▓(c ÔÇó 1) = 0` (when `Z Ôëá 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_neg` | `Var_╬▓(ÔêÆA) = Var_╬▓(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add_const_smul_one` | `Var_╬▓(A + c ÔÇó 1) = Var_╬▓(A)` (when `Z Ôëá 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq_symm_add_half_commutator` | `Cov_╬▓(A, B) = Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) + (1/2) ┬À Ôƒ¿A ┬À B ÔêÆ B ┬À AÔƒ®_╬▓` (symmetric / antisymmetric decomposition) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_eq_half_add_swap` | `Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) = (1/2) ┬À (Cov_╬▓(A, B) + Cov_╬▓(B, A))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq_symm_of_commute` | for commuting `A, B`, `Cov_╬▓(A, B) = Cov^s_╬▓(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_conjTranspose_swap_of_isHermitian` | `star Tr(¤ü ┬À X) = Tr(¤ü ┬À Xß┤┤)` for Hermitian `¤ü` (generic helper) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_star_swap_of_isHermitian` | `star Ôƒ¿A ┬À BÔƒ®_╬▓ = Ôƒ¿B ┬À AÔƒ®_╬▓` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | `(Ôƒ¿A┬ÀB + B┬ÀAÔƒ®_╬▓).im = 0` (anticommutator is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | `(Ôƒ¿A┬ÀB ÔêÆ B┬ÀAÔƒ®_╬▓).re = 0` (commutator is purely imaginary) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | `(Ôƒ¿H ┬À OÔƒ®_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

### Heisenberg chain (Tasaki ┬º3.5)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬º3.5, p. 89.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf G J` | the canonical pairwise coupling `╬ø ÔåÆ ╬ø ÔåÆ Ôäé` of a `SimpleGraph G` with uniform edge weight `J`: returns `J` on edges of `G`, zero otherwise (graph-centric bridge) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf_symm` / `_self` / `_real` | symmetry (from `G.Adj` symmetry), vanishing on the diagonal (from irreflexivity), and reality (for real edge weight) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.pathGraph_adj_iff` / `cycleGraph_adj_iff` | path / cycle graph adjacency in the explicit `x.val + 1 = y.val Ôê¿ ...` form used elsewhere in the codebase | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+1) ÔåÆ Fin (N+1) ÔåÆ Ôäé`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds, zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+2) ÔåÆ Fin (N+2) ÔåÆ Ôäé`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds (mod N+2), zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the open-chain coupling is `couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the periodic-chain coupling is `couplingOf (cycleGraph (N+2)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_isHermitian_of_real_symm` | for any real symmetric coupling `J` the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = ╬ú_{x,y} J(x,y) ┼£_x ┬À ┼£_y` is Hermitian | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_couplingOf_isHermitian` | **graph-centric** Hermiticity: for any `SimpleGraph G` and real edge weight `J : Ôäé`, the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` is Hermitian. The chain instances are corollaries via the bridge theorems | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph G J` | named wrapper = `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` (parallel to `isingHamiltonianOnGraph`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | corollaries re-exposed under the named wrapper | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` / `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` | rfl bridges: chain Gibbs = graph Gibbs on pathGraph/cycleGraph | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_eq_isingGibbsStateOnGraph` | chain Ising Gibbs = `isingGibbsStateOnGraph (pathGraph (N+1)) ╬▓ (-J/2) h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | the periodic Ising Gibbs state commutes with the periodic Ising Hamiltonian (free corollary of `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsExpectation_zero` / `_im_of_isHermitian` / `_commutator_hamiltonian` / `_hamiltonian_im` / `_hamiltonian_pow_im` / `isingCycle_partitionFn_im` / `_ofReal_re_eq` / `isingCycleGibbsState_pow_trace` | periodic-Ising expectation companions of the open-chain `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation*` family: ╬▓ = 0 closed form, real-valuedness for Hermitian observables, conservation `Ôƒ¿[H, A]Ôƒ® = 0`, energy / energy-power expectations real, partition-function real, real-cast `((Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®_╬▓).re : Ôäé) = Ôƒ¿OÔƒ®_╬▓`, R├®nyi-n trace `Tr(¤ü_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

| `hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity / commute corollaries | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |

| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_eq` | for `N = 1` (the 2-site open chain on `Fin 2`), `H_open = -2J ┬À spinHalfDot 0 1` (explicit one-bond reduction; Tasaki ┬º2.4 simplest concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=1) ┬À |ÔåæÔåæÔƒ® = -(J/2) ┬À |ÔåæÔåæÔƒ®` ÔÇö `|ÔåæÔåæÔƒ®` lies in the `S = 1` triplet sector and is an exact eigenvector with eigenvalue `-J/2` (this is the ferromagnetic ground state for `J < 0`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_singlet` | `H_open(N=1) ┬À (|ÔåæÔåôÔƒ® - |ÔåôÔåæÔƒ®) = (3J/2) ┬À (|ÔåæÔåôÔƒ® - |ÔåôÔåæÔƒ®)` ÔÇö singlet eigenvalue, the antiferromagnetic ground state for `J > 0` (Tasaki ┬º2.5 concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `H_open(N=1) ┬À |ÔåôÔåôÔƒ® = -(J/2) ┬À |ÔåôÔåôÔƒ®` ÔÇö all-down state has the same eigenvalue as all-up (both are `S = 1` triplet states) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_triplet_zero` | `H_open(N=1) ┬À (|ÔåæÔåôÔƒ® + |ÔåôÔåæÔƒ®) = -(J/2) ┬À (|ÔåæÔåôÔƒ® + |ÔåôÔåæÔƒ®)` ÔÇö triplet `m = 0` state, completing the 3-fold degenerate triplet representation `S = 1` with eigenvalue `-J/2` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_eq` | for `N = 2` (the 3-site open chain on `Fin 3`, 2 bonds), `H_open = -2J ┬À (spinHalfDot 0 1 + spinHalfDot 1 2)` ÔÇö explicit two-bond reduction | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=2) ┬À |ÔåæÔåæÔåæÔƒ® = -J ┬À |ÔåæÔåæÔåæÔƒ®` ÔÇö confirming the linear scaling `E(|Ôåæ..ÔåæÔƒ®) = -N┬ÀJ/2` (here `N = 2` bonds, `J = 1` per bond) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_sum_eq` | for any `N : Ôäò`, `╬ú_{x,y Ôêê Fin (N+1)} openChainCoupling N J x y = -(2N ┬À J : Ôäé)` (the bond-counting lemma: each of the `N` unordered nearest-neighbour bonds is counted in both orientations) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | for any `N : Ôäò`, `J : ÔäØ`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H_open ┬À |s..sÔƒ® = -(N┬ÀJ/2 : Ôäé) ┬À |s..sÔƒ®` ÔÇö both `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) share the same eigenvalue by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `s = 0` specialisation of the above (Tasaki ┬º2.4 (2.4.5)/(2.4.1) ferromagnetic ground-state energy `E_GS = -|B|┬ÀS┬▓` for `S = 1/2`, `|B| = N` bonds) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `s = 1` specialisation: same eigenvalue `-(N┬ÀJ/2)` for the all-down state by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_all_up` | for any `N : Ôäò`, `J : ÔäØ`, `k : Ôäò`, `H_open ┬À ((┼£tot^-)^k ┬À |Ôåæ..ÔåæÔƒ®) = -(N┬ÀJ/2 : Ôäé) ┬À ((┼£tot^-)^k ┬À |Ôåæ..ÔåæÔƒ®)` ÔÇö the unnormalised Tasaki ┬º2.4 (2.4.9) ferromagnetic ground states `|╬ª_MÔƒ®` made explicit on the chain (combines PRs #82 + #98) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_all_down` | dual ladder from `|Ôåô..ÔåôÔƒ®`, same eigenvalue `-(N┬ÀJ/2)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_open` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_periodic` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

### Perron-Frobenius theorem (`Math/PerronFrobenius.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean`, `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean`)

Perron-Frobenius theorem for nonneg irreducible/primitive matrices (Issue #405, closed).
The sorry in `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` is eliminated via the Collatz-Wielandt port (PRs AÔÇôC).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.IsPrimitive.of_irreducible_pos_diagonal` | irreducible nonneg + positive diagonal ÔåÆ primitive (Seneta ┬º1.1, Prop. 1.3, p. 17) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.collatzWielandtFn` | CW function `min_{i\|x_i>0} (Ax)_i/x_i` (Seneta ┬º1.2, p. 27) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_any_ratio` | `CW(x) Ôëñ (Ax)_i/x_i` for `x_i > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_mulVec` | fundamental inequality `CW(x)┬Àx Ôëñ Ax` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.smul_eq` | scale invariance `CW(cx) = CW(x)` for `c > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.upperSemicontinuousOn` | CW is upper-semicontinuous on stdSimplex | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.exists_maximizer` | CW attains its max on stdSimplex (EVT for USC, Seneta ┬º1.2, p. 28) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.eq_eigenvalue` | `CW(v) = r` when `Av = r┬Àv`, `v > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.lt_of_all_ratios_gt` | all ratios `> c` Ôƒ╣ `CW(x) > c` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.pos_of_nonneg_eigenvec` | irreducible nonneg + `Av = ╬╝v`, `v ÔëÑ 0`, `v Ôëá 0` Ôƒ╣ `v > 0` (standard propagation argument) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_primitive` | primitive nonneg Ôƒ╣ Ôêâ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ┬º1.2, pp. 27ÔÇô28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_irreducible` | irreducible nonneg Ôƒ╣ Ôêâ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ┬º1.2, pp. 27ÔÇô28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `exists_nonneg_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry**, retained for docs) symmetric nonneg max eigenvalue has nonneg eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry-free**) irreducible nonneg Hermitian Ôƒ╣ max eigenvalue has strictly positive eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `pos_eigenvec_unique` | strictly positive eigenvector unique up to positive scalar | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `PerronFrobenius.eigenvec_proportional_of_pos_eigenvec` | **geometric simplicity of the Perron eigenvalue**: irreducible nonneg with a strictly positive eigenvector `v` at `╬╝` Ôƒ╣ every real eigenvector at `╬╝` is `s ÔÇó v` for some scalar `s` (the `╬╝`-eigenspace is 1-dimensional). Perturbation trick: `v + t ÔÇó u > 0` for small `t > 0` is an eigenvector at `╬╝`, hence `ÔêØ v` by `pos_eigenvec_unique`, forcing `u ÔêØ v`. Used to show a Perron ground state is a joint eigenvector of any commuting operator (e.g. the total Casimir) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusSimple.lean` |

References: E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains* (3rd ed.), Springer 2006, ┬º1.2 (pp. 27ÔÇô28);
or4nge19/MCMC: `MCMC/PF/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PerronFrobenius/`.

### Spin-`S` MarshallÔÇôLiebÔÇôMattis on the magnetization sector (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.2 generic S, sector form)

> **Route correction (2026-05-26).** The earlier "saturated-ladder-iterate"
> route toward `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` was found **unsound**: it reduced the
> theorem to a leaf hypothesis (lowerable attach-sum dominance) that is **false**
> ÔÇö the ferromagnetic ladder iterate `(┼£Ôü╗_tot)^M|all-upÔƒ®` is *not* the
> Marshall-positive antiferromagnetic ground state (concrete counterexample:
> spin-1/2, `V={a,b}`, `A={a}`, `B={b}`, `M=0ÔåÆ1`). The 40 conditional-wrapper
> modules of that route were removed; their detailed rows below were deleted with
> them. The **sound** route keeps the per-sector PerronÔÇôFrobenius ground state
> (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`, sorry-free) and chains sectors by
> SU(2) invariance `[─ñ, ┼£Ôü╗_tot] = 0`; some rows below still reference helper
> lemmas from the removed route pending re-documentation. See
> `.self-local/docs/tasaki-2-5-pf-route-design.md` and Issue #3542.

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.2 applied to the
**magnetization-`M` sector** of the un-dressed antiferromagnetic
Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a bipartite graph. The sector subtype
`magConfigS V N M := { ¤â : V ÔåÆ Fin (N + 1) // magSumS ¤â = M }` is the
natural index type since the dressed Heisenberg matrix is irreducible
on each sector. The core sector-matrix API lives in
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSector.lean`; the trailing
eigenvalue-uniqueness + complex-form MLM Theorem 2.2 statements
were moved to the companion
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSectorEigenvalueUnique.lean` in
refactor #30 (PR #2829). Tracked in Issue #412.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (lowering)**: if a magnetization-`M` sector vector `magSectorEmbedding ╬ª` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at energy `╬╝` and a total-Casimir eigenvector at `╬│` away from the sector's lowering-kernel value `(card┬ÀN/2ÔêÆM)(card┬ÀN/2ÔêÆMÔêÆ1)`, then `┼£Ôü╗_tot ┬À magSectorEmbedding ╬ª` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at the **same** `╬╝` (via `[─ñ, ┼£Ôü╗_tot]=0`), is non-zero (total-Casimir non-vanishing criterion), and lies in the next magnetization sector. This is the energy-preserving step that chains the per-sector PerronÔÇôFrobenius ground states (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`) across the admissible range; it uses **no** Marshall positivity of the lowered vector (the false hypothesis of the removed ferromagnetic-ladder route). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred` / `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred_of_mem_predictedGS` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (raising)** and its predicted-GS specialisation: the raising companions of `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ(_of_mem_predictedGS)`. `┼£Ôü║_tot` maps a sector-`K` Heisenberg eigenvector (total-Casimir away from the raising-kernel value `(card┬ÀN/2ÔêÆK)(card┬ÀN/2ÔêÆK+1)`) to a non-zero same-energy eigenvector in the previous sector; predicted-GS membership in sector `M+1` strictly above the left endpoint discharges the Casimir hypotheses via `tasaki23_predictedCasimirValue_ne_raising_kernel_value_of_mem_of_left_lt`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagSubspaceS` / `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS` (with `mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_iff`, `basisVecS_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`, `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_disjoint`) | **Sublattice magnetization grading**: the `┼£_A^(3)`-eigenspaces `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` and the diagonal eigenvalue `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ¤â = Ôêæ_{xÔêêA}(N/2ÔêÆ¤â_x)`. Submodule API (zero/add/smul/neg/sub/finite-sum membership), disjointness of distinct eigenvalues, and basis-state membership. Sublattice analogue of `magSubspaceS`/`magEigenvalueS`; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 5) for the sublattice highest-weight argument bounding `(┼£_A)┬▓`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagnetization.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_sublattice_casimir_re_le` | **Sublattice Casimir bounds for the toy ground state**: the per-sector Marshall-positive toy GS is a joint Casimir eigenvector (#3657) whose `(┼£_A)┬▓`/`(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` eigenvalues `╬│_A, ╬│_B` obey `╬│_A.re Ôëñ s_A(s_A+1)`, `╬│_B.re Ôëñ s_B(s_B+1)` (apply the spectral max bound #3672 to the non-zero Marshall-dressed embedding). Discharges two hypotheses of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 3). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToySublatticeBounds.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_extremal_sector_magnetization_re_eq_predicted` | **Extremal sector realizes the predicted total spin**: for the left-endpoint sector `M = min(|A|,|┬¼A|)┬ÀN`, the magnetization eigenvalue `|V|┬ÀN/2 ÔêÆ M` has real part `= tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N = |s_A ÔêÆ s_B|`. Supplies the extremal-sector hypothesis `hM` of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 4). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ExtremalSector.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_of_energy_le` | **Toy ground state is the predicted-Casimir witness (modulo the energy bound)**: in the extremal sector `M = min(|A|,|┬¼A|)┬ÀN`, the Marshall-positive toy GS with toy energy `╬╝ Ôëñ predicted ÔêÆ s_A(s_A+1) ÔêÆ s_B(s_B+1)` is a `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at exactly `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N`. Capstone assembling the joint eigenvector (#3657), sublattice bounds (#3677), toy energy formula (#3673), extremal sector (#3678), the two-sided pin (#3676), and Hermitian eigenvalue realness ÔÇö exactly the witness form required by #3656. The single remaining obligation is the reference energy bound `h╬╝` (Issue #3674, PR 5). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyWitness.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_sector_groundEnergy_le_of_witness` | **Toy ground energy is minimal in its sector**: the Marshall-positive toy ground state (energy `╬╝`) has `╬╝ Ôëñ E` for every sector eigenvector `¤å Ôëá 0` of the toy Heisenberg sector matrix at real energy `E`. Specialises `heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector_eigenvalue_ge_of_marshallPositive` to `J = bipartiteCoupling A`; reduces the reference energy bound `h╬╝` (and hence the whole route, via #3679) to exhibiting one sector eigenvector at the predicted ground energy `predicted ÔêÆ s_A(s_A+1) ÔêÆ s_B(s_B+1)` (Issue #3674, PR 6). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyGroundEnergyBound.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_total_casimir_re_eq_predicted_of_bounds` (with `tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin_nonneg`) | **Toy total-Casimir pin (extremal sector)**: in the extremal magnetization sector `M.re = |s_AÔêÆs_B|`, a non-zero `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│_tot` with sublattice Casimir max bounds `╬│_A Ôëñ s_A(s_A+1)`, `╬│_B Ôëñ s_B(s_B+1)` and reference toy energy bound `(╬│_totÔêÆ╬│_AÔêÆ╬│_B).re Ôëñ predicted ÔêÆ s_A(s_A+1) ÔêÆ s_B(s_B+1)` has `╬│_tot.re = predicted`. A two-sided squeeze (magnetization lower bound #3675 + sublattice upper bounds #3672 + energy formula #3673), isolating the extremal-sector and reference-energy obligations as hypotheses; no ClebschÔÇôGordan (Issue #3674, PR 2). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyCasimirPin.lean`) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_ge_of_mem_magSubspaceS` (with `exists_highestWeight_eigenvector_ge`) | **Total-Casimir magnetization lower bound** `(┼£_tot)┬▓ ÔëÑ m(m+1)`: a non-zero `(┼£_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in `magSubspaceS V N M` with `0 Ôëñ M.re` has `M.re┬À(M.re+1) Ôëñ ╬│.re`. The total spin dominates its `z`-projection: raising with `┼£Ôü║_tot` only increases the magnetization, so the highest weight `M'` reached has `M' ÔëÑ M`, giving `╬│ = M'(M'+1) ÔëÑ M(M+1)`. Lower-bound companion of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; in the extremal sector `m = s_AÔêÆs_B` it pins the toy GS total Casimir to predicted, bypassing the ClebschÔÇôGordan triangle inequality (Issue #3674, PR 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirSpectralLowerBound.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_of_joint_casimir_eigenvector` | **Toy energy on a joint Casimir eigenvector**: a simultaneous eigenvector of `(┼£_tot)┬▓`, `(┼£_A)┬▓`, `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` at `(╬│_tot, ╬│_A, ╬│_B)` is a `─ñ_toy`-eigenvector at `╬│_tot ÔêÆ ╬│_A ÔêÆ ╬│_B` (from `─ñ_toy = (┼£_tot)┬▓ÔêÆ(┼£_A)┬▓ÔêÆ(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓`). The energy formula underlying the variational identification of the toy ground state's predicted total Casimir (Issue #3658, PR 4 step 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianJointEnergy.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvalue_re_le_sA` | **Sublattice Casimir spectral max bound** `(┼£_A)┬▓ Ôëñ s_A(s_A+1)`: every eigenvalue `╬│` of `(┼£_A)┬▓` (with a non-zero eigenvector) has `╬│.re Ôëñ s_A(s_A+1)`, `s_A = |A|┬ÀN/2`. Assembles the sublattice weight-component extraction (#3670), highest-weight existence (#3671), and the highest-weight bound (#3668). Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; the sublattice analytic ingredient of the Issue #3658 witness construction (Issue #3658, PR 12). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirSpectralBound.lean`) |
| `sublatticeExists_highestWeight_eigenvector` (with `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_ne_sMax_add_one`) | **Sublattice highest-weight existence**: from a non-zero `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A (s_AÔêÆk)`, repeatedly raising with `┼£_A^+` (which terminates once the sublattice magnetization exceeds `s_A`, since the subspace at `s_A+1` is `ÔèÑ`) produces a non-zero highest-weight `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at the same `╬│`. Sublattice analogue of `exists_highestWeight_eigenvector` (Issue #3658, PR 11). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeHighestWeightExistence.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvec_exists_weight_component` (with `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_sublatticeMagProjFn_eq`, `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`) | **Sublattice weight-component extraction**: since `(┼£_A)┬▓` commutes with `┼£_A^(3)` it preserves each sublattice magnetization subspace and commutes with the projector; hence a non-zero `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` has a non-zero sublattice-weight component `sublatticeMagProjFn A (s_AÔêÆk) v` that is again a `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in a single sublattice magnetization subspace. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvec_exists_weight_component` (Issue #3658, PR 10). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagWeightComponent.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagProjFn` / `sum_sublatticeMagProjFn_eq` (with `sublatticeMagSumS`, `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_eq_sub`, `sublatticeMagProjFn_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`) | **Sublattice magnetization projection & decomposition**: the pointwise projector `sublatticeMagProjFn A M v` (keeps `v ¤â` where `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ¤â = M`) lands in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M`, is linear, and the projectors over the finite spectrum `s_A ÔêÆ k` (`k=0..|A|┬ÀN`) sum to the identity (`sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ¤â = s_A ÔêÆ sublatticeMagSumS A ¤â`, `sublatticeMagSumS A ¤â = Ôêæ_{xÔêêA}¤â_x Ôëñ |A|┬ÀN`). Sublattice analogue of `magProjFn`/`sum_magProjFn_eq` (Issue #3658, PR 9). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagProjection.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_highestWeight_eigenvalue_re_le` (with `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_im_zero`) | **Sublattice highest-weight Casimir bound**: a non-zero highest-weight `(┼£_A)┬▓`-eigenvector (`┼£_A^+ w = 0`) at `╬│` in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` satisfies `╬│.re Ôëñ s_A(s_A+1)`, `s_A = |A|┬ÀN/2`. Its Casimir value is `M(M+1)` (#3663), `M` is an attained basis eigenvalue (#3667 ÔèÑ-lemma), so `|M| Ôëñ s_A` (#3660). Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_highestWeight_eigenvalue_re_le` (Issue #3658, PR 8). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirHighestWeightBound.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp3_apply_diag` / `sublatticeSpinSOp3_apply_off_diag` / `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_eq_bot_of_not_in_spectrum` | **Sublattice magnetization spectrum**: `┼£_A^(3)` is diagonal with entry `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ¤â` on the diagonal and zero off it; hence a value `M` outside the diagonal spectrum gives `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M = ÔèÑ`. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSOp3_apply_diag/off_diag` and `magSubspaceS_eq_bot_of_not_in_spectrum`; lets the highest-weight argument conclude an attained sublattice magnetization is an actual basis eigenvalue (so `|M| Ôëñ s_A`) (Issue #3658, PR 7). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagSpectrum.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_of_mem` / `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_of_mem` | **Sublattice ladder shifts the sublattice grading**: `┼£_A^+` maps `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M ÔåÆ A (M+1)` and `┼£_A^-` maps it to `A (MÔêÆ1)`, from the grading commutators `[┼£_A^(3), ┼£_A^┬▒] = ┬▒┼£_A^┬▒`. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem`; drives the termination of the highest-weight raising for `(┼£_A)┬▓` (Issue #3658, PR 6). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagShift.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_of_sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_eq_zero` | **Sublattice highest-weight Casimir relation**: if `┼£_A^(3) w = m_A┬Àw` and `┼£_A^+ w = 0`, then `(┼£_A)┬▓ w = m_A(m_A+1)┬Àw`. From the sublattice Cartan identity `┼£_A^- ┼£_A^+ = (┼£_A^1)┬▓+(┼£_A^2)┬▓ÔêÆ┼£_A^(3)` (so `(┼£_A)┬▓ = ┼£_A^-┼£_A^+ + ┼£_A^(3) + (┼£_A^(3))┬▓`); on a highest-weight `┼£_A^(3)`-eigenvector the `┼£_A^-┼£_A^+` term vanishes. Sublattice analogue of the total highest-weight relation; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 4) for the sublattice Casimir spectral max bound. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeHighestWeight.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_of_sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_eq_zero` | **Sublattice lowest-weight Casimir relation**: if `┼£_A^(3) w = m_A┬Àw` and `┼£_A^- w = 0`, then `(┼£_A)┬▓ w = m_A(m_AÔêÆ1)┬Àw`. From the other sublattice Cartan identity `┼£_A^+ ┼£_A^- = (┼£_A^1)┬▓+(┼£_A^2)┬▓+┼£_A^(3)` (so `(┼£_A)┬▓ = ┼£_A^+┼£_A^- ÔêÆ ┼£_A^(3) + (┼£_A^(3))┬▓`); on a lowest-weight vector the `┼£_A^+┼£_A^-` term vanishes. At `m_A = ÔêÆs_A` this gives `(┼£_A)┬▓ = s_A(s_A+1)` (max), so the all-down sublattice state realizes the maximal `(┼£_A)┬▓` ÔÇö a building block of the minimal-total-spin state (Issue #3674). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeLowestWeight.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_antiAligned_eq_max` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_complement_antiAligned_eq_max` (with `antiAlignedConfigS`) | **Anti-aligned state realizes the maximal sublattice Casimirs**: the basis config `antiAlignedConfigS A N` (all `A`-sites up `¤â=0`, all `┬¼A`-sites down `¤â=N`) satisfies `(┼£_A)┬▓ |¤âÔéÇÔƒ® = s_A(s_A+1) |¤âÔéÇÔƒ®` and `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓ |¤âÔéÇÔƒ® = s_B(s_B+1) |¤âÔéÇÔƒ®` ÔÇö the `A`-block is the sublattice highest weight (#3663), the `┬¼A`-block the sublattice lowest weight (#3681). The bipartite toy Hamiltonian's per-sector PerronÔÇôFrobenius ground state (`exists_marshallSign_eigenvector_heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector`) has energy `╬╝ Ôëñ E = predicted ÔêÆ s_A(s_A+1) ÔêÆ s_B(s_B+1)` via the predicted-energy sector eigenvector (#3710) and `tasaki23_toy_sector_groundEnergy_le_of_witness` (#3680); lifting to the full space and feeding `╬╝ Ôëñ E` into the witness capstone (#3656) pins its total Casimir to the predicted value. **This discharges the single remaining obligation of the sound PerronÔÇôFrobenius route** (Issue #3658), supplying the `hw_cas` witness for the overlap pin `tasaki23_pf_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_of_witness`. The toy witness (#3711) supplies the predicted-Casimir state in the same sector, and the overlap pin `tasaki23_pf_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_of_witness` (Tasaki eq. 2.5.12) transfers the value to the `J`-ground state. This discharges the `hsource_cas` hypothesis of the sector-existence chain at the base sector (Issue #3542). Inspection of the ladder link (`tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ`/`_pred`) shows only the total-Casimir eigenvalue (vs. the lowering/raising-kernel value) is consumed; the sublattice components are never used. This matters for the sound route because the ground state of an arbitrary bipartite `J` carries the predicted *total* Casimir (overlap pin) but need not be a sublattice-Casimir eigenvector, so it is not in the joint subspace. Hence `╬╝_M = ╬╝_{M+1}` for two adjacent Marshall-positive PF ground states sharing the predicted total Casimir (Issue #3542). | `tasaki23_jointPredicted_lowering_succ` | **Lowering a joint predicted-Casimir eigenvector to the next sector**: a non-zero sector-`M` (admissible, below the right endpoint) simultaneous eigenvector of `(┼£_tot)┬▓` (at the predicted value), `(┼£_A)┬▓` (at `╬│_A`), `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` (at `╬│_B`) lowers under `┼£Ôü╗_tot` to a non-zero simultaneous eigenvector at the same three eigenvalues. Since `┼£Ôü╗_tot` commutes with all three Casimir operators (`totalSpinSSquared_commute_totalSpinSOpMinus`, `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_totalSpinSOpMinus`) the eigenvalues are preserved, and lowering is non-vanishing because the predicted value differs from the sector's lowering-kernel value. Iterating from the extremal base sector spreads the joint predicted-Casimir line across the whole admissible range ÔÇö the Casimir-controlled propagation that avoids any lowered Marshall-positivity input (Issue #3542). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23JointPredictedLowering.lean`) |
| `exists_jointPredictedCasimir_embed_base`, `exists_jointPredictedCasimir_embed_sector` | **A joint predicted-Casimir eigenvector in every admissible sector** (for `|┬¼A| Ôëñ |A|`): in each `M Ôêê tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N = [min(|A|,|┬¼A|)┬ÀN, max(|A|,|┬¼A|)┬ÀN]` there is a non-zero `magSectorEmbedding ╬ª` that is a simultaneous eigenvector of `(┼£_tot)┬▓` (at the predicted value), `(┼£_A)┬▓` (at `s_A(s_A+1)`) and `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓` (at `s_B(s_B+1)`). The base sector `M = min(|A|,|┬¼A|)┬ÀN` re-derives #3709 in embedded form; the general sector is reached by induction on the distance from the base, lowering with `tasaki23_jointPredicted_lowering_succ` (#3714) and re-embedding via `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem`. This is the per-sector predicted-Casimir input the constancy chain (#3713) and overlap pin (#3712) consume, obtained without any lowered Marshall-positivity ("site-sum") hypothesis (Issue #3542). | `tasaki23_toy_min_energy_arith` | **Arithmetic core of the toy minimum-energy bound** (option (a) toward interior-sector predicted Casimir): for real spin values `0 Ôëñ a Ôëñ s_A`, `0 Ôëñ b Ôëñ s_B Ôëñ s_A`, `f(a,b) := |aÔêÆb|(|aÔêÆb|+1) ÔêÆ a(a+1) ÔêÆ b(b+1)` is minimised at the corner `(s_A, s_B)`, i.e. `f(s_A, s_B) Ôëñ f(a, b)`. Combined with the coupled total-spin lower bound `(┼£_tot)┬▓ ÔëÑ |aÔêÆb|(|aÔêÆb|+1)` on the joint sublattice-Casimir eigenspace `W_{a,b}` (the ClebschÔÇôGordan triangle inequality, in progress), this gives `─ñ_toy ÔëÑ bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted` on each `W_{a,b}`, hence the global toy minimum-energy bound; see `.self-local/tex/3716-tasaki-2-5-toy-min-energy-bound.tex` (Issue #3542). | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_re_ge_sublatticeSpinSOp3_mul_succ`, `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_re_ge_sublatticeSpinSOp3_mul_pred` | **Sublattice Casimir dominates `S┬│(S┬│┬▒1)`** (option (a), Route 5 brick 0): for a simultaneous eigenvector `v Ôëá 0` of `(┼£_A)┬▓` (eigenvalue `╬│`) and `┼£_A^(3)` (eigenvalue `q`), `(q(q+1)).re Ôëñ ╬│.re` and `(q(qÔêÆ1)).re Ôëñ ╬│.re`. From the Cartan identities `(┼£_A)┬▓ = ┼£_A^Ôêô ┼£_A^┬▒ + ┼£_A^(3)(┼£_A^(3)┬▒1)` and the positive semidefiniteness `Ôƒ¿v, ┼£_A^Ôêô ┼£_A^┬▒ vÔƒ® = ÔÇû┼£_A^┬▒ vÔÇû┬▓ ÔëÑ 0` (using `(┼£_A^┬▒)^ÔÇá = ┼£_A^Ôêô`). This is the magnitude bound (`|q| Ôëñ b` on the `(┼£_A)┬▓=b(b+1)` eigenspace) that excludes spurious sublattice weights at the coupled total-spin lower bound's recurrence boundary; see `.self-local/tex/3717-coupled-total-spin-lower-bound.tex` (Issue #3542). | `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_eq_zero_sublatticeSpinSOp3_re_nonpos` | **A sublattice lowest weight has non-positive magnetization** (option (a), Route 5 brick 1): if `v Ôëá 0`, `┼£_A^(3) v = q v` and `┼£_A^- v = 0`, then `q.re Ôëñ 0`. The lowest-weight Casimir relation `(┼£_A)┬▓ = q(qÔêÆ1)` plus the magnitude bound `(┼£_A)┬▓ ÔëÑ q(q+1)` (brick 0) force `q(qÔêÆ1) ÔëÑ q(q+1)`, i.e. `q.re Ôëñ 0`. This pins the unique admissible lowest-weight magnetization (`q = ÔêÆb`, excluding the spurious root `q = b+1`) at the recurrence boundary of the coupled total-spin lower bound (Issue #3542). | `sublatticeMagProjFn_of_mem`, `sublatticeMagProjFn_of_mem_ne`, `sublatticeMagProjFn_mulVec_of_commute`, `sublatticeMagProjFn_sublatticeSpinSquaredS`(`_complement`) | **Sublattice magnetization-projection infrastructure** (option (a), Route 5 brick 2a): the pointwise sublattice-`A` magnetization projector `sublatticeMagProjFn A M` is idempotent on its own subspace, annihilates other subspaces, and commutes with any operator commuting with `┼£_A^(3)` (proved via the magnetization-level decomposition `sum_sublatticeMagProjFn_eq`). Hence the magnetization component of an `(┼£_A)┬▓`- (resp. `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓`-) eigenvector is again a Casimir eigenvector at the same eigenvalue ÔÇö the Casimir-inheritance needed for the bottom-component extraction of the coupled total-spin lower bound (Issue #3542). | `exists_sublattice_A_lowestWeight_component` | **Bottom sublattice-`A` magnetization component is `┼£_A^-`-killed** (option (a), Route 5 brick 2b): a total lowest-weight `w` (`┼£Ôü╗_tot w = 0`, total magnetization `m`, simultaneous sublattice-Casimir eigenvector at `╬▒, ╬▓`) yields a non-zero `┼£_A^-`-killed simultaneous weight vector `v` with `┼£_A^(3) v = p v`, `┼£_┬¼A^(3) v = (mÔêÆp) v` and the inherited Casimir eigenvalues `╬▒, ╬▓`. Obtained as the minimal-`A`-magnetization nonzero component (max over the magnetization-level projections): projecting `┼£Ôü╗_tot w = ┼£_A^- w + ┼£_┬¼A^- w = 0` onto the level just below `p` leaves only `┼£_A^- v` (the `┼£_┬¼A^-` part preserves `A`-magnetization and sees only the vanishing lower component). | `totalLowestWeight_re_le_complement_sub` | **One-sided total-magnetization bound** (option (a), Route 5 brick 3a): a total lowest weight `w` in the joint sublattice-Casimir eigenspace with spins `a, b` (`(┼£_A)┬▓ = a(a+1)`, `(┼£_┬¼A)┬▓ = b(b+1)`) has `m.re Ôëñ b ÔêÆ a`. The bottom-`A`-component (#brick 2b) is `┼£_A^-`-killed at `A`-weight `p`, so `p Ôëñ 0` (brick 1) and `p(pÔêÆ1) = a(a+1)` (lowest-weight Casimir) force `p Ôëñ ÔêÆa`; the magnitude bound on `┬¼A` (brick 0) gives `m ÔêÆ p Ôëñ b`, whence `m.re Ôëñ b + p.re Ôëñ b ÔêÆ a`. This is the reduction to the lowest weight for the coupled total-spin lower bound (Issue #3542). Since `(┼£_A)┬▓ = ┼£_A^(1)┬▓ + ┼£_A^(2)┬▓ + ┼£_A^(3)┬▓` is a sum of squares of Hermitian operators, `Ôƒ¿v, (┼£_A)┬▓ vÔƒ® = ╬ú_╬▒ ÔÇû┼£_A^(╬▒) vÔÇû┬▓ ÔëÑ 0`. | `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_normSq_eq`, `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_ne_zero_of_pos_weight`, `totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_ne_zero_of_neg_weight` | **One total-spin ladder step is non-vanishing on a weight vector** (general-`J` `hOutside` thread, step 1, PR #3736): from `[┼£Ôü║_tot, ┼£Ôü╗_tot] = 2 ┼£┬│_tot` (the two Casimir rearrangements) and `(┼£Ôü╗)ÔÇá = ┼£Ôü║`, a weight-`w` vector `╬ª` (`┼£┬│_tot ╬ª = w ╬ª`) satisfies `ÔÇû┼£Ôü╗_tot ╬ªÔÇû┬▓ = ÔÇû┼£Ôü║_tot ╬ªÔÇû┬▓ + 2 w ÔÇû╬ªÔÇû┬▓`. Apply the anticommutation `╬ÿ ┼£┬│_tot = -┼£┬│_tot ╬ÿ` (#3744) to ╬ª: get `╬┤╬│ ÔÇó ╬ª = -╬│╬┤ ÔÇó ╬ª` Ôƒ╣ `2╬┤╬│ ÔÇó ╬ª = 0` Ôƒ╣ `╬┤╬│ = 0` Ôƒ╣ `╬│ = 0` (since ╬┤ Ôëá 0). The Marshall sign product equals `ÔêÆ1` on any `┬▒1` move on the `A`-site (#3760), flipping the strict-positive bond entry (#3790) to strict-negative. Given `U * Uinv = 1`, `Uinv * U = 1`, `H' = Uinv * H * U`, AND `Q * U = U * Q`, then `finrank Ôäé (eig H' ╬╝ Ôèô ker(Q=c)) = finrank Ôäé (eig H ╬╝ Ôèô ker(Q=c))`. The core technical step for transferring per-parity-block PF bounds from the dressed `─ñ'_dressed` to the bare `─ñ'`, since the Marshall sign diagonal `╬ÿ_A` commutes with the diagonal `magParityDiagS P`. | `bare_axisSwapped_full_eigenspace_inter_parity_finrank_le_one` (with `marshallDiagonal_commute_magParityDiagS`) | **(g.4) bare `─ñ'` per-block PF `finrank Ôäé Ôëñ 1` via Marshall similarity** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3836): transfers the dressed `─ñ'_dressed` per-block bound (#3834) to the bare axis-swapped `─ñ'` via the Marshall similarity for intersected eigenspaces (#3835). Key observation: the Marshall sign diagonal `╬ÿ_A` and the magnetization parity diagonal `P = magParityDiagS` are both diagonal and hence commute, so the intersection `eig ─ñ' ╬╝ Ôèô ker(P=c)` is preserved by the Marshall similarity dressed ÔåÆ bare. Result for `p < 2`: `Ôêâ ╬¢ : ÔäØ, finrank Ôäé (eig (axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS J lam D N) (╬¢ : Ôäé) Ôèô ker(P=(-1)^p)) Ôëñ 1`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BareAxisSwapFullEigInterParityLeOne.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one` (with `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one`) | **(g.5) bare anisotropic `─ñ` eigenspace `finrank Ôäé Ôëñ 2` (conditional, via axis-swap)** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3837): wraps the conditional `bare ─ñ' eig Ôëñ 2 from per-block Ôëñ 1` (`axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one`, #3776 family) with the axis-swap unitary equivalence (`AxisSwapUnitaryS.anisotropic_axisSwapped_eigenspace_finrank_eq`, #3753) to give the same conditional bound for the bare anisotropic Hamiltonian `─ñ`. General `N` version requires an explicit `AxisSwapUnitaryS N` instance; spin-1/2 (`N = 1`) instance via `axisSwapUnitarySpinHalf`. This **closes the bare `─ñ` Ôëñ 2 link in the (g) chain**, conditional on per-parity-block PF hypotheses (discharged separately per sector by #3836). The remaining gap to unconditional obligation (1) is the ╬¢-sector matching (PF eigenvalues of even/odd sectors coinciding at the ground-state energy), which is mathematically distinct (Hermitian spectral minimisation) and deferred. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BareAnisotropicEigLeTwoConditional.lean`) |
| `parity_block_submatrix_full_inter_finrank_eq` (with `parity_block_submatrix_eigenspace_finrank_le_full_inter`, `parityEmbedMap`) | **(h.1) reverse block-diag bridge** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3840): for a parity-block-diagonal complex `M`, the parity-`p` submatrix's ╬¢-eigenspace embeds INJECTIVELY into `eig M ╬¢ Ôèô ker(P=(-1)^p)` via the zero-extension map (extend by 0 off `parityConfigS p`). Combined with the forward direction (#3832), gives the finrank equality `finrank Ôäé (eig M.submatrix ╬¢) = finrank Ôäé (eig M ╬¢ Ôèô ker(P=(-1)^p))` for `p < 2`. The equality unlocks ╬¢-sector matching arguments: per-block submatrix eig finrank equals the full eig Ôèô ker(P) finrank, so per-sector PF simplicity (#3834/#3836) directly transfers to constraints on the FULL eigenspace at that energy. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BlockDiagSubmatrixEmbed.lean`) |
| `eigenspace_finrank_eq_of_commuting_involution` | **(h.2) involution eigenspace decomposition ÔÇö finrank EQUALITY** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3841): strengthens `eigenspace_finrank_le_of_commuting_involution` (#3776) from `Ôëñ` to `=`. For finite-dim `T, P` with `TÔêÿP = PÔêÿT` and `PÔêÿP = id`, the `T`-eigenspace at any `╬╝` splits as a **direct sum** across the two `P`-eigenspaces `┬▒1`. The `Ôëñ` was tight because the two intersected `┬▒1` eigenspaces of `P` are disjoint (a single eigenvector cannot have two distinct `P`-eigenvalues), so `Submodule.finrank_sup_add_finrank_inf_eq` collapses to a sum (inf = 0). Used by (h.3) to derive the block-sum equality. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/InvolutionEigenspaceDecompEq.lean`) |
| `matrix_eigenspace_finrank_eq_sum_parity_blocks` (with `dressedAxisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_..._sum_parity_blocks`, `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_..._sum_parity_blocks`) | **(h.3) block-sum finrank equality** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3842): combines (h.1) #3840 and (h.2) #3841 to give the block-sum equality for any parity-block-diagonal `M` commuting with `magParityDiagS`: `finrank Ôäé (eig M ╬╝) = finrank Ôäé (eig M.submatrix_0 ╬╝) + finrank Ôäé (eig M.submatrix_1 ╬╝)`. Specialised to the dressed `─ñ'_dressed` and the bare `─ñ'` (both block-diagonal and commuting with `P`). Central tool for unconditional `Ôëñ 2` at any ╬╝ where both per-block bounds hold simultaneously. Combined with PF (per-sector simplicity at the per-sector spectrum minimum) and a Hermitian spectral identification of the GS energy as `min(╬¢_0, ╬¢_1)`, gives the unconditional bare `─ñ'` GS degeneracy `Ôëñ 2`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BlockSumFinrankEq.lean`) |
| `matrix_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_submatrix_blocks_le_one` (with `dressedAxisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_..._of_submatrix_blocks`, `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_..._of_submatrix_blocks_le_one`, `anisotropicHeisenbergS_..._of_submatrix_blocks_le_one`, `spinHalf_..._of_submatrix_blocks_le_one`) | **(h.4) Ôëñ 2 wrappers from per-block submatrix simplicity** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3844): wraps (h.3) #3842 into easier-to-use forms for consumers of per-sector PF simplicity. Hypotheses stated on the SUBMATRIX eigenspaces (directly produced by #3831/#3836), bypassing the eig Ôèô ker(P) intersection form. 5 theorems: generic block-diag wrapper, dressed ─ñ', bare ─ñ', bare ─ñ via AxisSwapUnitaryS N, and spin-1/2 specialisation. Proofs via (h.3) sum equality + omega; the bare anisotropic ─ñ versions additionally use the axis-swap unitary equivalence (#3753 family). | `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_isHermitian_of_real` (with `dressedAxisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_isHermitian_of_real`) | **(i.1) parity-block submatrix Hermiticity** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3846): the parity-`p` submatrix of the bare and dressed axis-swapped Hamiltonians inherits Hermiticity from the full Hamiltonian via `Matrix.IsHermitian.submatrix`. Starting point for Hermitian spectral analysis of the per-sector matrix ÔÇö needed to identify the per-sector PF eigenvalue as the spectrum minimum and ultimately bridge the conditional Ôëñ 2 (g/h chains) to the unconditional ground-state degeneracy bound. | `hermitianMinEigenvalue` (with `hermitian_min_eigenvalue_mem_image`, `hermitian_min_eigenvalue_le`, `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_min_eigenvalue`, `dressedAxisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_min_eigenvalue`) | **(i.3) submatrix minimum eigenvalue (Hermitian)** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3848): for a Hermitian matrix `M` on a non-empty finite-dim space, the minimum of its eigenvalue function (`Matrix.IsHermitian.eigenvalues`) is well-defined as a real number via `Finset.min'`. | `hermitian_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_min` | **(i.4) Hermitian eigenspace = ÔèÑ below min eigenvalue** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3849): for a Hermitian matrix `M` on a finite-dim complex space, if `╬╝ < hermitianMinEigenvalue M`, then the eigenspace at `(╬╝ : Ôäé)` is `ÔèÑ`. Proof via spectrum-eigenvalues bridge (`HasEigenvalue.mem_spectrum` ÔåÆ `Matrix.spectrum_toLin'` ÔåÆ `spectrum.algebraMap_mem_iff` ÔåÆ `IsHermitian.spectrum_real_eq_range_eigenvalues`). Rules out eigenvalues below the minimum. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43ÔÇô44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianEigenspaceBotBelowMin.lean`) |

Given per-block bounds `finrank Ôëñ 1` at the per-block minimum eigenvalues, the full-eigenspace at `min(min_0, min_1)` has `finrank Ôëñ 2`. Case analysis: `min_0 = min_1` both contribute `Ôëñ 1`; `min_0 Ôëá min_1` the higher block contributes `0` by (i.4) #3849, the lower `Ôëñ 1`. Bridge from per-block PF simplicity to the unconditional global ground-state degeneracy `Ôëñ 2` (modulo identification of PF eigenvalue with `hermitianMinEigenvalue`). | `axisSwappedParityBlockPFMinAt` / `axisSwappedParityBlockPFMinPath` / `axisSwappedParityBlockStrictRawSupportPath` / `axisSwappedParityBlockLambdaOneRawSupportPath` / `axisSwappedParityBlockDZeroRawSupportPath` / `exists_parityBlock_dressed_diag_strict_upper_bound` / `axisSwappedParityBlockStrictRawSupportPath_of_reachability` / `axisSwappedParityBlockLambdaOneRawSupportPath_of_reachability` / `axisSwappedParityBlockDZeroRawSupportPath_of_reachability` / `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_finrank_le_one_at_full_min_of_pf_min` / `caseII_axisSwapped_submatrix_blocks_path_of_pf_min` / `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_pf_min_path_of_caseII_raw_support` / `caseII_axisSwapped_parityBlockPFMinPath_of_raw_support` / `caseII_axisSwapped_submatrix_blocks_path_of_raw_support_pf_min` / `caseII_axisSwapped_parityBlockPFMinPath_of_reachability` / `caseII_axisSwapped_submatrix_blocks_path_of_reachability_pf_min` / `caseII_coupling_eq_zero_of_not_bipartiteCompleteGraph_adj` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_block_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_block_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_raw_support_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_raw_support_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_reachability_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_reachability_pf_min_path` | **General spin-S case-(ii) target bridge from pathwise parity-block PF/min callbacks** (Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): turns the previous row's full-ground-energy parity-block simplicity input into the existing bare-submatrix conditional form. For each parity block, if a PF eigenvalue `╬¢` has `finrank <= 1` and equals that block's Hermitian minimum, then the block has `finrank <= 1` at the full anisotropic ground energy: if the full ground energy equals the block minimum, this is the conditional theorem; if it is lower, `hermitian_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_min` makes the block eigenspace zero. The path wrapper supplies both parity blocks along `╬│(t)`, and the target wrappers feed the result to the block-path bridge above. The raw-support abbreviations name the strict, `lambda = 1`, and `D = 0` reachability/PF inputs for one parity block. The one-block selector chooses among the fixed raw-support consumers by the case-(ii) path inequalities and leaves only the corner `(lambda,D)=(1,0)` PF/min callback explicit; the even/odd raw-support wrapper applies this selector to both parity blocks, and the full-min wrapper feeds those callbacks into the block-path PF/min transfer. The raw-support target wrappers derive the support-zero hypothesis from `hJself` and `hJbip`, then expose target uniqueness and zero magnetization directly from the raw-support input surface. The reachability wrappers use finite parity blocks to supply strict diagonal shifts automatically, so the public reachability-level target wrappers require only the even and odd strict, ion-only, and bond-only reachability totality hypotheses plus the two explicit corner callbacks. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIBlockPFMin.lean`) |

The raise/lower and parity-bond positivity inputs only require `D.re ÔëÑ 0`, so the parity-block irreducibility, global `finrank Ôäé Ôëñ 2`, deformation contradiction, balanced-sector ground-energy equality, and final target uniqueness/zero-magnetization wrappers extend from `D > 0` to `D ÔëÑ 0` while retaining `-1 < ╬╗ < 1`. The named callback adapter exposes this site-sum form as the API consumed by the new interval chain and final theorem wrapper: predicted-GS membership supplies the re-embedded source-weight identity and lowered sublattice-Casimir/support data at each successor step, the predecessor-difference callback is converted to strict lowered site-sum positivity, and the direct site-sum successor chain finishes the common-energy proof without first converting the boundary to the raising-source dominance final callback. The callback receives the source predicted-GS membership, the identity applying `┼£_A^+` and `┼£_┬¼A^+` to the two lowered components, and both lowered-component memberships in `tasaki23LoweredJointMagSubspace A N M`. This isolates the remaining comparison under exactly the cross-ladder equation and subspace structure already proved for predicted toy ground states. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (files `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PredictedLadder.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23IntervalJoint.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23OutsideGroundCrossLadderLoweredJointCross.lean`) |
| `tasaki23JointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace` / `tasaki23_energy_interval_chain_with_predictedGS_of_left_endpoint_predictedGS_of_joint_sublattice_component_lt_of_predictedGS` | **Joint-component callback for Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3**: the joint maximum sublattice-Casimir eigenspace used by the remaining comparison is now named in `Theorem23PredictedLadder.lean`, and the predicted-GS interval chain passes the exact structural facts into the local comparison callback. At each successor step the callback receives the propagated `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` membership plus the two facts `┼£_A^- ╬ª Ôêê tasaki23JointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace A N` and `┼£_┬¼A^- ╬ª Ôêê tasaki23JointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace A N`, then returns the strict pointwise Marshall-signed component inequality. This directly connects the joint-eigenspace bridges to the final local comparison obligation. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (files `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PredictedLadder.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23IntervalJoint.lean`) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_totalSpinSOpMinus_pow_of_eigenvec` / `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_totalSpinSOpPlus_pow_of_eigenvec` / `totalSpinSOpMinus_pow_mulVec_ne_zero_of_casimir_ne_kernel_values` / `totalSpinSOpPlus_pow_mulVec_ne_zero_of_casimir_ne_kernel_values` | **Iterated total-spin ladder Casimir preservation and non-vanishing for Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3**: the preservation lemmas transport a total-Casimir eigenvalue through an iterated `┼£_tot^-` or `┼£_tot^+` ladder. The non-vanishing lemmas then prove that the iterated ladder output remains nonzero whenever every intermediate magnetization avoids the corresponding one-step ladder-kernel Casimir value. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23Local.lean`) |

The complex-form `marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_complexGroundState_full`
is the COMPLEX-Hilbert-space form of Tasaki ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.2 in the
magnetization sector: the ground state of the un-dressed quantum
Heisenberg Hamiltonian restricted to the sector is unique (up to a
positive real scalar in its real part) and has the Marshall sign
structure `╬ª ¤â := ((sign A ¤â.1).re * v ¤â : Ôäé)` with `v > 0`.

`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` (PR #3337) is the **final-statement form**
of the `|A| Ôëá |┬¼A|` case. The hypothesis bundle matches
`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergHamiltonianS_groundState_full`
(#869) exactly. The conclusion bundles, per admissible sector
`M Ôêê tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N`, the Marshall-positive
sector eigenvector existence and within-sector uniqueness from
#869, together with a global energy-minimality clause. The
predicted total spin and predicted degeneracy enter as the
cardinality identity `|tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N| =
tasaki23PredictedDegeneracy A N = 2 S_tot + 1` with
`S_tot = ||A| ÔêÆ |┬¼A||┬ÀN/2 = tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N`. The
proof iterates #869 across `tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N`.

References: H. Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, Springer 2020, ┬º2.5 Theorem 2.2 (pp. 39ÔÇô43), Theorem 2.3
(p. 42); E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains*
(3rd ed.), Springer 2006, ┬º1.2 (pp. 27ÔÇô28) for the underlying
PerronÔÇôFrobenius theorem.

### Spin-`S` saturated ferromagnetic state (Tasaki ┬º2.4 generalised)

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki ┬º2.4 P1i for the
**saturated ferromagnet**: the all-aligned (constant-spin) basis
state `|¤â_ÔèñÔƒ® = Ôèù_x |cÔƒ®` with `¤â x = c` for all `x : V`. The two
extremal weights `c = 0` (highest weight, "all up") and
`c = Fin.last N` (lowest weight, "all down") are the highest- and
lowest-weight vectors of the `J_tot = |V|┬ÀS = |V|┬ÀN/2` irreducible
SU(2) representation in the multi-site Hilbert space. Tracked in
Issue #412; assembled in PRs #875ÔÇô#879. All theorems live in
`Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedState.lean`.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
